title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Issues and Projects
summary:
Learn how to get organized and collaborate more efficiently with this step-by-step tutorial.

The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Issues and Projects appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
content:
Welcome back to GitHub for Beginners, our series designed to help you navigate GitHub like a pro and get the most out of it! We’re bringing you a double dose by sharing these episodes in video format and adding them to our blog, so you can consume the material in whichever form works better for you!

We’re now entering our third season! In the previous seasons, we focused on a general introduction to all things Git and then spent an entire season talking about Copilot. This season, we’re going back to the basics and exploring the core features of GitHub. Our goal is to help you level up—or dust off—your GitHub skills.

In this episode, we’re going to start with two of GitHub’s most powerful collaboration tools: GitHub Issues and Projects. By the end of this post, you’ll know how to create an issue, how to sync your issues to a GitHub Project board, and how to use GitHub Projects to track your work.

Let’s get into it! Or, watch the full video above!

Why issues and projects matter

GitHub Issues and Projects are essential tools for any team that wants to stay organized and collaborate efficiently. Issues help you track tasks, bugs, and ideas, all in a clear shared space—ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. Projects look at the bigger picture, letting you visualize, prioritize, and plan your work, turning individual issues into an actionable workflow that everyone can follow.

By combing issues and projects, you can coordinate work, communicate progress, and ensure that everyone’s aligned toward the same goal.

Creating your first GitHub Issue

GitHub Issues are a simple way to capture anything that needs attention in your project, whether that’s a new idea, a bug, or a task. Think of them as the building blocks of what you want to add to or update in your project. They help teams work collaboratively and stay organized, while making sure that every item is addressed.

So how do you create an issue? Here’s a sample repository where you can try this out for yourself by following these steps.

  1. Navigate to gh.io/gfb-issues in your browser. 
  2. At the top of the page, click the Issues tab. 
  3. In the top-right of the window, click the green New issue button. 
  4. A window will appear with a couple of boxes. First, give your issue a clear title by entering it in the box under “Add a title.” 
  5. Provide a description for your issue in the “Add a description” box. Your description should include all of the relevant information necessary to set expectations. When someone attempts to resolve this issue, what is the behavior they should add or fix? 
  6. On the right-hand side of the window, you have several options that you can configure.  
    • Assignee: Assign someone to work on this issue directly. You can even assign yourself to show what you’re working on. 
    • Labels: Classify your issue into one of several predesigned categories or potentially add a new one. 
    • Type: Indicate whether your issue is about something like a bug or a task. 
    • Projects: Add your issue to projects, which we’ll be covering later in this post. 
    • Milestone: Assign your issue to a specific milestone. 
  7. Click the green Create button below the issue description. 

Congratulations! You have just created your first issue! Once an issue is created, anyone on the team can jump in and comment. All they need to do is enter some text in the comment field and then click the Comment button. You can even link issues together by typing # and then the number of the issue in the comment field. GitHub will automatically provide a clickable link to the numbered issue. This is super handy for tracking related work.

And when the work is finished, you can close the issue, letting the entire team know that it’s been addressed.

Creating your first GitHub Project

Projects give you a way to group GitHub Issues together in a visual dashboard that’s perfect for planning, organizing, and tracking work. It’s a tool for breaking big goals down into manageable tasks that you can track all in one place.

Now let’s walk through creating a project board. Here’s a sample repository where you can try this out, or you can do it in your own repository. If you still have the repository open from the walkthrough above, you can skip the first step.

  1. Navigate to gh.io/gfb-issues in your browser.
  2. At the top of the page, click the Projects tab.
  3. In the top-right of the window, click the green New project button.
  4. A window will pop up providing several templates for you to start from. Select the Kanban template.
  5. Enter a title in the “Project name” field.
  6. Unselect the checkbox for Bulk import items.
  7. At the bottom of the window, select Create project.

GitHub will now create the project for you and take you to the project view. You’ll see the name of your project at the top of the window. In addition, GitHub automatically created columns for you, but you can customize them however you want.

Notice that underneath your project’s name, there are several tabs. These correspond to different views of the project board. Feel free to click on them to see what the different views look like in your project.

To manage your project board, click the three dots in the top-right corner and select Settings. This opens a new page where you can:

  • Manage who has access to your project board.
  • Create custom fields.
  • Update any current fields.
  • Change your project name.
  • Provide or change the project description.
  • Include a README.
  • Create a copy of your project board.
  • Change the visibility of your project.

To customize charts

At the top of the window, select the Insights button. On the following page, you’ll be able to view, create, and customize charts. You can change the layout of any of your charts by clicking the Configure button and choosing options from the dropdown menus.

To check the status of workflows

Going back to the main window, you can see a Workflows button next to the Insights button on the top. Select the Workflows button. Now you can see several built-in workflows on the left side of the window that you can use to update the status of items based on certain events. For example, you can automatically set the status to todo when an item is added to your project, close issues when the issue’s status in your project is changed or set the status to Done when an issue is closed.

To add a status update

Going back to the main project view once again, select the Add status update button at the very top. This opens a window where you can add a status report of your project’s health and progress.

Connecting issues and projects

Combining issues and projects together is how you’re able to get a complete view of your work. Think of issues as individual tasks, and projects as the dashboard that organizes those tasks. When you link issues to a project board, you can visualize where everything stands at a glance.

Go ahead and open your project board if it isn’t open already. At the bottom of the window, select Add item. You can either create a new issue right here, or you could search for existing ones. Click the plus icon and select Add item from repository. Select the issue or issues that you want to add to this project board. You can use the checkbox at the top of the list to automatically select all items at once if you want to add them all.

Once you’ve selected the issues you want to add, press the Add elected items button at the bottom of the window. Notice how this adds all of the selected issues to your project board as cards. You can select the cards and move them between status columns as work progresses. You can also click on the issue to see the full details of the issue.

And here’s something cool. The issue and your project board are now synchronized. When you change the status of the issue in one, it will automatically be reflected in the other. You can move multiple issues at once, organizing them into columns that match your workflow, and get that satisfying feeling when you finally move them into the “Done” column.

End-to-end flow

Let’s now simulate a real workflow that you might use in your team project by going through the following steps.

  1. Navigate to your project board if you are not already there.
  2. Click Add item at the bottom of your first column, indicating that you are adding a new issue to your workflow.
  3. Click the plus icon and select Create new issue from the context menu.
  4. Enter a title and description for the issue, describing a new feature you want to add.
  5. Select Create to create the issue and automatically add it to the project board.
  6. Underneath the description, select Labels and select enhancement from the list of options.
  7. Click the issue in the project board to open it.
  8. In the right-hand column, click the gear next to “Assignees” to assign this issue to someone on your team.
  9. Add comments to the issue to signify making progress or providing updates.
  10. Now let’s say that some work was done to address this issue. Open a pull request, and in the comment field, mention that it closes the relevant issue. Do this by entering Closes # and then the number connected with the issue in the pull request description. This will automatically connect to the issue and provide a link to it for ease of reference.
  11. Merge the pull request, and it will automatically close the issue.

This workflow keeps everyone aligned without the need for constant status meetings. Everything you need to know is there, both in the issue and on the project board!

What’s next?

Now you know how to use GitHub Issues and Projects to organize your work with GitHub. Start simple with a few issues, build out your project board as you go, and you’ll have a system that scales with your team.

If you want to learn even more about GitHub Issues and GitHub Projects, make sure to check out our documentation.

Happy coding!

The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Issues and Projects appeared first on The GitHub Blog.

GitHub发布新手教程，手把手教你使用核心的“问题”和“项目”功能来管理团队工作。

title: Multi-Sourced, Multi-Agent Evidence Retrieval for Fact-Checking

content:
arXiv:2603.00267v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Misinformation spreading over the Internet poses a significant threat to both societies and individuals, necessitating robust and scalable fact-checking that relies on retrieving accurate and trustworthy evidence. Previous methods rely on semantic and social-contextual patterns learned from training data, which limits their generalization to new data distributions. Recently, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based methods have been proposed to utilize the reasoning capability of LLMs with retrieved grounding evidence documents. However, these methods largely rely on textual similarity for evidence retrieval and struggle to retrieve evidence that captures multi-hop semantic relations within rich document contents. These limitations lead to overlooking subtle factual correlations between the evidence and the claims to be fact-checked during evidence retrieval, thus causing inaccurate veracity predictions.
  To address these issues, we propose WKGFC, which exploits authorized open knowledge graph as a core resource of evidence. LLM-enabled retrieval is designed to assess the claims and retrieve the most relevant knowledge subgraphs, forming structured evidence for fact verification. To augment the knowledge graph evidence, we retrieve web contents for completion. The above process is implemented as an automatic Markov Decision Process (MDP): A reasoning LLM agent decides what actions to take according to the current evidence and the claims. To adapt the MDP for fact-checking, we use prompt optimization to fine-tune the agentic LLM.
aiSummary: 针对网络虚假信息泛滥的挑战，研究人员提出了一种名为WKGFC的新型事实核查方法。该方法创新性地利用权威开放知识图谱作为核心证据源，并引入大语言模型驱动的智能体进行多步骤推理检索，以捕捉证据与待核查声明之间复杂的语义关联。相比传统依赖文本相似度的检索方法，WKGFC能更有效地发现多跳语义关系，从而提升事实核查的准确性和泛化能力。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，这项研究就像给事实核查装上了‘超级大脑’和‘知识地图’。它不再只是简单匹配关键词，而是能像侦探一样，根据线索（待核查信息）在庞大的知识库和互联网中层层推理，找到最相关、最可靠的证据来验证真假。
aiInterpretation: 在虚假信息对个人与社会构成严重威胁的背景下，开发强大且可扩展的事实核查技术至关重要。传统方法依赖从训练数据中学习的语义和社交上下文模式，难以泛化到新的数据分布。近期基于检索增强生成的方法虽能利用大语言模型的推理能力，但其证据检索主要依赖文本相似度，难以捕捉文档内容中复杂的多跳语义关系，导致在证据检索阶段可能忽略证据与待核查声明之间细微的事实关联，从而影响核查准确性。 为什么重要： 这项研究的重要性在于，它直接针对当前事实核查技术的关键瓶颈——证据检索的深度与关联性不足。通过结构化知识图谱与动态网络内容检索的结合，该方法有望更精准地锚定事实，为打击虚假信息提供了更可靠的技术路径。 影响面： 从技术层面看，该方法融合了知识图谱、大语言模型智能体和马尔可夫决策过程，推动了多模态、多智能体检索推理技术的发展。在商业层面，更精准的事实核查工具对内容平台、新闻媒体和社交媒体公司的内容治理与风险控制具有重要价值。产业链方面，可能催生对高质量知识图谱构建、智能体开发与优化服务的需求。在监管与安全领域，该技术能为政府机构、非营利组织提供更高效的信息验证手段，助力网络空间清朗。对用户与社会而言，有助于公众更便捷地获取可信信息，提升整体信息素养与社会信任度。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
这是一条关于人工智能如何更智能地进行事实核查的科技新闻。研究人员开发了一个新系统，能像侦探破案一样，通过结合知识地图和网络搜索，一步步推理出证据来验证信息的真假。

[翻成人话]
现在的AI查证信息，有点像只靠关键词对答案，容易漏掉深层联系。新方法让AI学会了‘顺藤摸瓜’：先看问题，然后去权威知识库里找相关线索，再去网上补充细节，通过多步骤推理，最终拼凑出完整证据来判断信息真伪。

[为什么重要]
因为网上假消息太多，危害大。传统查证方法不够聪明，新方法能让AI查得更深、更准，对我们每个人辨别信息、平台管理内容、乃至维护健康的网络环境都很有帮助。

[下一步确认点]
这个新系统的实际效果如何？它处理各种复杂、隐蔽假信息的能力真的比现有方法强吗？未来有没有计划把它做成我们普通人也能用的工具？
fullText: Misinformation spreading over the Internet poses a significant threat to both societies and individuals, necessitating robust and scalable fact-checking that relies on retrieving accurate and trustworthy evidence. Previous methods rely on semantic and social-contextual patterns learned from training data, which limits their generalization to new data distributions. Recently, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based methods have been proposed to utilize the reasoning capability of LLMs with retrieved grounding evidence documents. However, these methods largely rely on textual similarity for evidence retrieval and struggle to retrieve evidence that captures multi-hop semantic relations within rich document contents. These limitations lead to overlooking subtle factual correlations between the evidence and the claims to be fact-checked during evidence retrieval, thus causing inaccurate veracity predictions. To address these issues, we propose WKGFC, which exploits authorized open knowledge graph as a core resource of evidence. LLM-enabled retrieval is designed to assess the claims and retrieve the most relevant knowledge subgraphs, forming structured evidence for fact verification. To augment the knowledge graph evidence, we retrieve web contents for completion. The above process is implemented as an automatic Markov Decision Process (MDP): A reasoning LLM agent decides what actions to take according to the current evidence and the claims. To adapt the MDP for fact-checking, we use prompt optimization to fine-tune the agentic LLM

title: TraderBench: How Robust Are AI Agents in Adversarial Capital Markets?
summary:
arXiv:2603.00285v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Evaluating AI agents in finance faces two key challenges: static benchmarks require costly expert annotation yet miss the dynamic decision-making central to real-world trading, while LLM-based judges introduce uncontrolled variance on domain-specific tasks. We introduce TraderBench, a benchmark that addresses both issues. It combines expert-verified static tasks (knowledge retrieval, analytical reasoning) with adversarial trading simulations score
content:
arXiv:2603.00285v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Evaluating AI agents in finance faces two key challenges: static benchmarks require costly expert annotation yet miss the dynamic decision-making central to real-world trading, while LLM-based judges introduce uncontrolled variance on domain-specific tasks. We introduce TraderBench, a benchmark that addresses both issues. It combines expert-verified static tasks (knowledge retrieval, analytical reasoning) with adversarial trading simulations scored purely on realized performance-Sharpe ratio, returns, and drawdown-eliminating judge variance entirely. The framework features two novel tracks: crypto trading with four progressive market-manipulation transforms, and options derivatives scoring across P&L accuracy, Greeks, and risk management. Trading scenarios can be refreshed with new market data to prevent benchmark contamination. Evaluating 13 models (8B open-source to frontier) on ~50 tasks, we find: (1) 8 of 13 models score ~33 on crypto with <1-point variation across adversarial conditions, exposing fixed non-adaptive strategies; (2) extended thinking helps retrieval (+26 points) but has zero impact on trading (+0.3 crypto, -0.1 options). These findings reveal that current agents lack genuine market adaptation, underscoring the need for performance-grounded evaluation in finance.
aiSummary: 研究人员推出金融AI测试平台TraderBench，旨在解决现有评估方法的不足。该平台结合专家验证的静态任务与对抗性交易模拟，通过夏普比率等实际绩效指标评分，完全消除了评判方差。在测试13个模型后发现，多数模型在加密交易中得分约33分且策略固定，缺乏真正的市场适应能力，突显了基于实际绩效评估的重要性。
aiExplanation: 简单说，研究人员造了个新“考场”来测试金融AI的水平。这个考场既有书本知识题，也有模拟真实市场波动的交易游戏，而且打分只看最终赚赔结果，非常公平。测试发现，现在的很多AI交易员其实不太会随机应变。

aiStocks.name: TraderBench
aiStocks.reason: 论文提出的金融AI评估基准

aiInterpretation: 为什么重要：在金融领域，评估AI智能体面临两大挑战：静态基准测试需要昂贵的专家标注，却无法捕捉真实交易中的动态决策；而基于大语言模型的评判者会在专业任务中引入不可控的方差。TraderBench基准的推出，通过结合专家验证的静态任务（如知识检索、分析推理）与纯粹基于实现绩效（如夏普比率、回报率、回撤）评分的对抗性交易模拟，有效解决了这些问题，为衡量AI在复杂市场环境中的真实能力提供了更可靠的框架。 影响面： - 技术：该基准引入了加密交易和期权衍生品两个新颖赛道，其中加密交易包含四级渐进的市场操纵变换，推动了AI模型在对抗性条件下的稳健性测试。 - 商业：研究结果揭示当前多数AI代理缺乏真正的市场适应能力，采用固定策略，这可能影响依赖AI进行自动化交易的投资机构的盈利能力和风险控制。 - 产业链：性能导向的评估需求可能促使AI开发商、数据提供商和金融机构加强合作，开发更动态的训练数据和模拟环境。 - 监管/安全：基准中对抗性模拟的设计，如市场操纵场景，有助于评估AI系统在潜在恶意市场行为下的表现，为相关监管和安全性考量提供参考。 - 用户/社会：更可靠的评估工具有助于提升金融AI产品的透明度和可信度，最终可能影响普通投资者对自动化交易工具的信任和使用。

研究人员发布了一个名为TraderBench的新测试平台，专门用来评估金融AI交易员的真实水平。
[翻成人话]
给AI交易员建了个更接近真实市场的“考场”，考它们知识储备和实战应变能力，结果发现很多AI表现僵化。
[为什么重要]
因为以前的测试方法要么太死板，要么打分标准不稳定。这个新方法能更真实地反映AI在复杂、甚至有人为操纵的市场里到底行不行，对开发可靠的金融AI至关重要。
[下一步确认点]
需要关注金融科技公司和AI实验室是否会采纳这个新基准来改进他们的模型，以及它如何影响未来自动化交易产品的设计和监管。

title: DIG to Heal: Scaling General-purpose Agent Collaboration via Explainable Dynamic Decision Paths
summary:
arXiv:2603.00309v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The increasingly popular agentic AI paradigm promises to harness the power of multiple, general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents to collaboratively complete complex tasks. While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent roles in order to reduce complexity, ideally these agents would be truly autonomous, able to achieve emergent collaboration even as the number of collaborating agents increases. Yet in practice, such un
content:
arXiv:2603.00309v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The increasingly popular agentic AI paradigm promises to harness the power of multiple, general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents to collaboratively complete complex tasks. While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent roles in order to reduce complexity, ideally these agents would be truly autonomous, able to achieve emergent collaboration even as the number of collaborating agents increases. Yet in practice, such unstructured interactions can lead to redundant work and cascading failures that are difficult to interpret or correct. In this work, we study multi-agent systems composed of general-purpose LLM agents that operate without predefined roles, control flow, or communication constraints, relying instead on emergent collaboration to solve problems. We introduce the Dynamic Interaction Graph (DIG), which captures emergent collaboration as a time-evolving causal network of agent activations and interactions. DIG makes emergent collaboration observable and explainable for the first time, enabling real-time identification, explanation, and correction of collaboration-induced error patterns directly from agents' collaboration paths. Thus, DIG fills a critical gap in understanding how general LLM agents solve problems together in truly agentic multi-agent systems. The project webpage can be found at: https://happyeureka.github.io/dig.
aiSummary: 研究人员提出了一种名为动态交互图（DIG）的新方法，旨在解决多AI智能体协作中的混乱问题。当多个通用大语言模型智能体在没有预设角色或流程的情况下协作时，常导致工作重复和错误扩散。DIG通过构建一个随时间演化的因果网络，首次让这种自发的协作过程变得可观察、可解释，并能实时识别和纠正协作错误。这项研究填补了理解智能体如何真正自主协作的关键空白。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，这就像给一群自由讨论、没有固定分工的AI助手装上了“对话地图”和“问题追踪器”。它能看清楚谁在什么时候、因为什么和谁交流，从而快速找到讨论跑偏或重复劳动的根源，并及时纠正。
aiInterpretation: 随着智能体AI范式的兴起，利用多个通用大语言模型智能体协作完成复杂任务成为趋势。理想情况下，这些智能体应能实现真正的自主协作，但随着协作智能体数量增加，缺乏结构化的交互常导致工作冗余和难以解释的级联故障。 为什么重要： 当前，许多多智能体系统依赖预定义的工作流程或角色来降低复杂性，但这限制了系统的自主性和灵活性。DIG的提出，首次使得在没有预设约束的、真正自主的智能体协作中，其动态交互过程变得可观察和可解释。这为理解和优化智能体间的自发协作提供了关键工具，是迈向更强大、更可靠自主AI系统的重要一步。 影响面： 从技术层面看，DIG提供了一种新的分析和调试框架，能实时识别协作错误模式，提升了多智能体系统的鲁棒性和可靠性。在商业应用上，更可靠的自主协作智能体可应用于更复杂的自动化流程，如客户服务、研发辅助等，提升效率。对产业链而言，这可能推动AI开发工具链的演进，催生新的监控、优化与调试服务。在监管与安全方面，可解释的协作路径有助于审计AI决策过程，增强透明度和可控性，符合AI治理要求。对用户与社会，更可靠、可理解的AI协作系统能减少错误输出，提升公众对AI的信任，并可能催生新的协同工作模式。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
一项关于AI智能体协作的新研究，提出了让多个AI自主协作过程变得透明、可解释的方法。
[翻成人话]
给一群自由发挥的AI助手开会装上了“会议记录仪”和“问题探测器”，能看清楚它们怎么商量、哪里出了岔子，并当场纠正。
[为什么重要]
这解决了多个AI一起干活时容易混乱、出错且难以追责的核心难题，是迈向真正可靠自主AI系统的关键一步。
[下一步确认点]
这项技术在实际复杂任务（如编程、科研分析）中的具体效果如何，以及它能否被方便地集成到现有的AI开发平台中。

While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent roles in order to reduce complexity, ideally these agents would be truly autonomous, able to achieve emergent collaboration even as the number of collaborating agents increases. Yet in practice, such unstructured interactions can lead to redundant work and cascading failures that are difficult to interpret or correct. title: How Well Do Multimodal Models Reason on ECG Signals?
summary:
arXiv:2603.00312v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While multimodal large language models offer a promising solution to the "black box" nature of health AI by generating interpretable reasoning traces, verifying the validity of these traces remains a critical challenge. Existing evaluation methods are either unscalable, relying on manual clinician review, or superficial, utilizing proxy metrics (e.g. QA) that fail to capture the semantic correctness of clinical logic. In this work, we introduce a
content:
arXiv:2603.00312v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While multimodal large language models offer a promising solution to the "black box" nature of health AI by generating interpretable reasoning traces, verifying the validity of these traces remains a critical challenge. Existing evaluation methods are either unscalable, relying on manual clinician review, or superficial, utilizing proxy metrics (e.g. QA) that fail to capture the semantic correctness of clinical logic. In this work, we introduce a reproducible framework for evaluating reasoning in ECG signals. We propose decomposing reasoning into two distinct, components: (i) Perception, the accurate identification of patterns within the raw signal, and (ii) Deduction, the logical application of domain knowledge to those patterns. To evaluate Perception, we employ an agentic framework that generates code to empirically verify the temporal structures described in the reasoning trace. To evaluate Deduction, we measure the alignment of the model's logic against a structured database of established clinical criteria in a retrieval-based approach. This dual-verification method enables the scalable assessment of "true" reasoning capabilities.
aiSummary: 研究人员开发了一个新框架，用于评估多模态大语言模型在心电图信号上的推理能力。该框架将推理分解为‘感知’和‘演绎’两个部分，分别通过代码验证和临床知识库比对进行客观评估。这解决了现有评估方法要么依赖人工、要么流于表面的问题，为验证医疗AI的‘黑箱’推理提供了可扩展的解决方案。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是科学家发明了一种新‘考试方法’，专门用来检查那些能看懂心电图的AI是不是真的会‘思考’。这套方法不仅看AI的答案对不对，还要一步步验证它得出答案的‘思路’是不是合理、符合医学常识。
aiInterpretation: 一项新研究提出了一个可复现的框架，专门用于评估多模态大语言模型在心电图信号上的推理能力。该框架的核心创新在于将复杂的临床推理过程分解为两个独立且可验证的组成部分：一是‘感知’，即从原始信号中准确识别出关键模式；二是‘演绎’，即基于领域知识对这些模式进行逻辑推断。为了评估‘感知’，研究采用了一种智能体框架，通过生成代码来实证检验模型在推理过程中所描述的时间结构是否正确。对于‘演绎’的评估，则通过检索比对的方式，将模型的逻辑与一个结构化的、包含既定临床标准的数据库进行对齐度测量。 为什么重要：在医疗AI领域，模型的‘黑箱’特性是阻碍其临床应用的重大障碍。虽然多模态大语言模型能够生成看似合理的推理过程，但其正确性难以保证。现有评估要么依赖耗时耗力的临床专家人工审核，要么使用问答准确率等表面指标，无法触及临床逻辑的语义核心。这项研究提出的双验证方法，为客观、可扩展地评估模型是否真正‘理解’并‘推理’提供了新工具，是推动可信赖医疗AI发展的关键一步。 影响面： - 技术：推动了医疗AI评估方法论的发展，从关注结果转向验证过程，提升了模型的可解释性与可信度。 - 商业：为开发更可靠的心电图分析AI产品提供了质量评估基准，可能加速此类产品通过监管审批并进入市场。 - 产业链：可能催生专注于AI医疗模型验证的新服务或工具，完善AI医疗产业链的中间环节。 - 监管/安全：为监管机构评估医疗AI的安全性、有效性和透明度提供了潜在的技术框架和参考标准。 - 用户/社会：最终有望让患者和医生使用到推理过程更透明、决策更可靠的AI辅助诊断工具，提升医疗质量与信任。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
科学家发明了新方法，给能‘读’心电图的AI做深度‘体检’，检查它是不是真会推理。

[翻成人话]
现在有些AI号称能分析心电图并给出理由，但它的‘思路’对不对很难说。新研究搞出了一套‘双保险’验证法：第一步，检查AI从心电图上‘看’到的关键点准不准；第二步，核对它根据这些点做出的医学判断逻辑通不通。

[为什么重要]
这直接关系到AI医疗靠不靠谱。如果只相信AI的最终答案，而不知道它怎么想的，就像让一个不解释原因的医生看病，风险很大。这套方法能大规模检验AI的‘思考过程’，是让它变得更透明、更可信的关键。

[下一步确认点]
这套评估框架能否推广到X光片、病理切片等其他医疗影像的分析AI上？它评估出的‘优秀推理AI’，在真实的临床诊断试验中，表现是否真的比传统AI更好？

Existing evaluation methods are either unscalable, relying on manual clinician review, or superficial, utilizing proxy metrics (e.g. QA) that fail to capture the semantic correctness of clinical logic. We propose decomposing reasoning into two distinct, components: (i) Perception, the accurate identification of patterns within the raw signal, and (ii) Deduction, the logical application of domain knowledge to those patterns. To evaluate Perception, we employ an agentic framework that generates code to empirically verify the temporal structures described in the reasoning trace. To evaluate Deduction, we measure the alignment of the model's logic against a structured database of established clinical criteria in a retrieval-based approach. This dual-verification method enables the scalable assessment of "true" reasoning capabilities

title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
summary:
arXiv:2603.00349v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable high-level cognitive coordination through reasoning, planning, and natural language communication. However, fine-grained analyses of how such collaboration emerges, unfolds, and contributes to task succe
content:
arXiv:2603.00349v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable high-level cognitive coordination through reasoning, planning, and natural language communication. However, fine-grained analyses of how such collaboration emerges, unfolds, and contributes to task success in embodied multi-agent systems are difficult to conduct with existing benchmarks. In this paper, we introduce EmCoop, a benchmark framework for studying cooperation in LLM-based embodied multi-agent systems. Our framework separates a high-level cognitive layer from a low-level embodied interaction layer, allowing us to characterize agent cooperation through their interleaved dynamics over time. Given a cooperation-constrained embodied task, we propose generalizable, process-level metrics that diagnose collaboration quality and failure modes, beyond final task success. We instantiate our framework in two embodied environments that scale to arbitrary numbers of agents and support diverse communication topologies, and use these instantiations to demonstrate how EmCoop enables systematic analysis of cooperation dynamics across team sizes and task settings. The project web page can be found at: https://happyeureka.github.io/emcoop.
aiSummary: 研究人员推出EmCoop框架与基准，旨在系统研究由大语言模型驱动的具身智能体在多智能体协作任务中的合作动态。该框架将高层认知与底层具身交互分离，并引入过程级指标来诊断协作质量与失败模式，超越了仅关注最终任务成功的传统评估。其已在两个可扩展的具身环境中实例化，支持分析不同团队规模与任务设置下的协作机制。
aiExplanation: 简单说，EmCoop就像给一群需要合作干活的机器人‘大脑’（大语言模型）设计了一套‘考试’和‘体检’系统。它不光看活干没干成，更仔细检查它们是怎么商量、怎么配合的，从而帮助科学家造出更会团队协作的机器人。
aiInterpretation: 随着现实世界任务日益复杂，许多工作已超出单个智能体的能力范围，需要多个具身智能体在动态环境中协作完成。尽管大语言模型在高层认知协调（如推理、规划和自然语言交流）方面展现出潜力，但现有基准难以对具身多智能体系统中协作如何产生、展开并促成任务成功进行细粒度分析。为此，研究团队提出了EmCoop，这是一个专门用于研究基于大语言模型的具身多智能体系统协作的基准框架。 为什么重要？ EmCoop的重要性在于它填补了当前研究的空白。现有评估多侧重于任务最终是否成功，而EmCoop通过将系统分离为高层认知层与底层具身交互层，能够刻画智能体随时间的交织动态，从而深入理解协作的内在过程。它提出的通用、过程级指标，可以诊断协作质量和失败模式，这对于开发更鲁棒、更高效的协作智能体系统至关重要。 影响面： 从技术层面看，EmCoop为评估和优化多智能体协作算法提供了标准化工具与量化指标，推动了具身人工智能在复杂场景下的技术发展。在商业应用上，更高效的协作智能体可提升仓储物流、服务机器人等领域的自动化水平与运营效率。对产业链而言，该框架可能催生新的仿真平台、工具链乃至专业服务需求。在监管与安全维度，对协作过程的深入分析有助于预见和防范多智能体系统可能出现的意外行为或安全风险。最后，对用户与社会的影响体现在，未来更智能、更协调的机器人助手能更好地融入人类生活与工作环境，提供更可靠的服务。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
研究人员发布了一个名为EmCoop的新框架和基准测试，专门用来研究和评估由大语言模型驱动的机器人（或称‘具身智能体’）如何更好地进行团队协作。

[翻成人话]
就是给一群需要一起干复杂活的机器人‘大脑’（大语言模型）设计了一套新的‘考试’和‘体检’标准。这套标准不光看最后活干没干成，更看重它们合作的过程配合得好不好，哪里容易出问题。

[为什么重要]
因为现实中的很多任务，比如物流分拣、灾难救援，单个机器人搞不定，必须多个机器人协作。现有的评估方法太看重结果，忽略了合作过程。EmCoop能深入分析协作的‘黑箱’，帮助科学家设计出更聪明、更会配合的机器人团队，这对未来自动化发展至关重要。

[下一步确认点]
1. 该框架在更复杂、更接近真实世界的任务场景中表现如何？
2. 基于此框架开发出的协作策略，能否直接迁移到物理实体机器人上？
3. 过程级评估指标如何与最终任务效率、成本等实际商业指标挂钩？
fullText: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable high-level cognitive coordination through reasoning, planning, and natural language communication. However, fine-grained analyses of how such collaboration emerges, unfolds, and contributes to task success in embodied multi-agent systems are difficult to conduct with existing benchmarks. In this paper, we introduce EmCoop, a benchmark framework for studying cooperation in LLM-based embodied multi-agent systems. Our framework separates a high-level cognitive layer from a low-level embodied interaction layer, allowing us to characterize agent cooperation through their interleaved dynamics over time. Given a cooperation-constrained embodied task, we propose generalizable, process-level metrics that diagnose collaboration quality and failure modes, beyond final task success. We instantiate our framework in two embodied environments that scale to arbitrary numbers of agents and support diverse communication topologies, and use these instantiations to demonstrate how EmCoop enables systematic analysis of cooperation dynamics across team sizes and task settings. The project web page can be found at: this https URL

title: Monotropic Artificial Intelligence: Toward a Cognitive Taxonomy of Domain-Specialized Language Models
summary:

Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in artificial intelligence research equates progress with scale: larger models trained on broader datasets are presumed to yield superior capabilities. content:
arXiv:2603.00350v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in artificial intelligence research equates progress with scale: larger models trained on broader datasets are presumed to yield superior capabilities. This assumption, while empirically productive for general-purpose applications, obscures a fundamental epistemological tension between breadth and depth of knowledge. We introduce the concept of \emph{Monotropic Artificial Intelligence} -- language models that deliberately sacrifice generality to achieve extraordinary precision within narrowly circumscribed domains. Drawing on the cognitive theory of monotropism developed to understand autistic cognition, we argue that intense specialization represents not a limitation but an alternative cognitive architecture with distinct advantages for safety-critical applications. We formalize the defining characteristics of monotropic models, contrast them with conventional polytropic architectures, and demonstrate their viability through Mini-Enedina, a 37.5-million-parameter model that achieves near-perfect performance on Timoshenko beam analysis while remaining deliberately incompetent outside its domain. Our framework challenges the implicit assumption that artificial general intelligence constitutes the sole legitimate aspiration of AI research, proposing instead a cognitive ecology in which specialized and generalist systems coexist complementarily.
aiSummary: 人工智能研究正挑战‘越大越好’的范式。一项新研究提出了‘单焦点人工智能’概念，主张开发极度专注于狭窄领域的语言模型，牺牲通用性以换取特定任务上的极致精确与安全。研究者借鉴了用于理解自闭症认知的‘单焦点主义’理论，并展示了一个仅3750万参数的小模型在特定工程分析任务上达到近乎完美的表现，而在其领域外则‘故意无能’。这为构建一个由专业与通用系统互补共存的AI认知生态提供了新思路。
aiExplanation: 简单说，这项研究认为AI不一定非要追求‘什么都懂’。就像有的专家只精通一门手艺反而做得更好一样，AI也可以极度专注于一个非常窄的领域（比如只分析一种工程结构），做到又快又准又安全，但在这个领域外它就‘装傻’。这为制造更可靠、更便宜的专用AI打开了新思路。
aiInterpretation: 当前人工智能研究的主流范式将进步等同于规模：更大的模型、更广的数据集被认为能带来更优的能力。然而，一项新研究挑战了这一假设，提出了‘单焦点人工智能’的概念。该概念主张开发一种语言模型，它们有意牺牲通用性，以在严格限定的狭窄领域内实现非凡的精确度。 为什么重要？ 这项研究的重要性在于，它从根本上质疑了将‘通用人工智能’视为AI研究唯一合法目标的隐含假设。通过借鉴理解自闭症认知的‘单焦点主义’认知理论，研究团队论证了极度专业化并非一种局限，而是一种具有独特优势的替代性认知架构，尤其适用于对安全性要求极高的关键应用场景。 影响面： 从技术角度看，它推动AI模型设计从一味追求规模转向深度专业化，可能催生更高效、更可靠的小型专家模型。在商业层面，这为在医疗诊断、金融分析、工业设计等垂直领域开发高精度、低风险的AI工具开辟了新路径。对产业链而言，可能降低大模型训练与部署的巨额成本，使更多中小企业能够应用尖端AI。在监管与安全维度，领域高度受限的模型因其‘故意无能’的特性，更容易进行安全验证和控制，降低了误用和不可预测风险。最后，对用户与社会，这意味着未来我们可能更频繁地与高度可靠、功能专一的AI助手交互，而非试图解决一切问题的‘通才’。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
AI研究新动向：不再盲目追求‘大而全’，转而打造‘极度专注’的专家型AI模型。

[翻成人话]
科学家认为，AI不一定非要学成‘万事通’。让AI像顶尖专家一样，只死磕一个非常窄的领域（比如只算某种桥梁的受力），反而能做得极其精准可靠，而且出了这个领域它就自动‘罢工’，这样更安全可控。

[为什么重要]
这挑战了‘通用人工智能才是终极目标’的流行观念。它证明，在某些对安全要求极高的关键任务上（如医疗、金融），一个功能单一但极度可靠的‘专家AI’，可能比一个什么都会但可能出错的‘通才AI’更有价值。这为AI发展提供了另一条务实路径。

[下一步确认点]
1. 这种‘专家AI’在更多复杂现实任务中的实际表现如何？
2. 如何高效地构建和管理大量不同领域的‘专家AI’？
3. 它的开发和应用成本是否真的大幅低于巨型通用模型？
4. 监管机构将如何评估和认证这类高度专业化AI的安全性？

title: Conservative Equilibrium Discovery in Offline Game-Theoretic Multiagent Reinforcement Learning
summary:
arXiv:2603.00374v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Offline learning of strategies takes data efficiency to its extreme by restricting algorithms to a fixed dataset of state-action trajectories. We consider the problem in a mixed-motive multiagent setting, where the goal is to solve a game under the offline learning constraint. We first frame this problem in terms of selecting among candidate equilibria. Since datasets may inform only a small fraction of game dynamics, it is generally infeasible in
content:
arXiv:2603.00374v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Offline learning of strategies takes data efficiency to its extreme by restricting algorithms to a fixed dataset of state-action trajectories. We consider the problem in a mixed-motive multiagent setting, where the goal is to solve a game under the offline learning constraint. We first frame this problem in terms of selecting among candidate equilibria. Since datasets may inform only a small fraction of game dynamics, it is generally infeasible in offline game-solving to even verify a proposed solution is a true equilibrium. Therefore, we consider the relative probability of low regret (i.e., closeness to equilibrium) across candidates based on the information available. Specifically, we extend Policy Space Response Oracles (PSRO), an online game-solving approach, by quantifying game dynamics uncertainty and modifying the RL objective to skew towards solutions more likely to have low regret in the true game. We further propose a novel meta-strategy solver, tailored for the offline setting, to guide strategy exploration in PSRO. Our incorporation of Conservatism principles from Offline reinforcement learning approaches for strategy Exploration gives our approach its name: COffeE-PSRO. Experiments demonstrate COffeE-PSRO's ability to extract lower-regret solutions than state-of-the-art offline approaches and reveal relationships between algorithmic components empirical game fidelity, and overall performance.
aiSummary: 研究人员提出了一种名为COffeE-PSRO的新方法，用于解决离线多智能体强化学习中的博弈问题。该方法在仅有固定历史交互数据、无法与环境实时交互的限制下，通过量化博弈动态的不确定性并引入保守主义原则，来寻找更可能接近真实博弈均衡的策略。实验表明，该方法比现有最先进的离线方法能发现更低遗憾值的解，性能更优。
aiExplanation: 简单说，这项研究就像教一群AI只用一本旧的棋谱学下棋，不能对弈练习。新方法能帮它们从有限的旧棋局里，更靠谱地猜出真正的好棋路，避免因为数据不全而学到歪招。
aiInterpretation: 在人工智能领域，多智能体强化学习旨在让多个智能体通过交互学习达成目标。然而，传统的在线学习方法需要大量实时交互，成本高昂。本研究聚焦于“离线”场景，即算法只能利用一组固定的历史交互轨迹数据来学习策略，这极大提升了数据效率，但也带来了巨大挑战：由于数据可能只揭示了博弈动态的一小部分，算法甚至难以验证一个候选策略是否是真正的均衡解。 为什么重要？ 这项研究的重要性在于，它直面了现实世界中一个普遍且棘手的难题：如何利用有限的、预先收集好的历史数据，来训练出能够进行有效协作或竞争的多智能体系统。例如，在训练自动驾驶汽车应对复杂交通场景，或分析历史金融市场数据以制定交易策略时，往往无法进行无限制的在线试错。本研究为这类“数据驱动”且“安全至上”的应用场景提供了新的理论基础和算法工具。 影响面： 从技术层面看，该方法创新性地将用于在线博弈求解的策略空间响应预言机框架与离线强化学习的保守主义原则相结合，提出了名为COffeE-PSRO的新算法，推动了多智能体学习算法的发展。在商业应用上，它能降低对高风险或高成本环境进行模拟训练的需求，加速机器人、自动驾驶等领域的算法开发与部署。对产业链而言，更高效、更安全的训练范式可能降低AI系统的开发门槛和运营成本。在监管与安全维度，离线学习避免了在线探索可能带来的不可控风险，符合对AI系统安全性、可预测性日益增长的监管要求。最后，对用户与社会，这意味着未来由AI驱动的协同服务或竞争系统可能更可靠、行为更可预期。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
人工智能研究新进展：提出了一种名为COffeE-PSRO的新算法，专门用于在“离线”条件下，即只能使用固定历史数据、不能实时试错的情况下，训练多个AI智能体进行博弈或协作。

[翻成人话]
以前训练AI对战或合作，得像打游戏一样不停尝试。新方法让AI能像考古一样，只靠过去留下的“行动记录”来学习，从中找出最靠谱的配合或竞争策略，不用再冒险试错了。

[为什么重要]
因为很多现实场景，比如训练自动驾驶车应对复杂路况，或者用历史金融数据训练交易AI，根本不允许你拿真车或真钱去反复冒险实验。这种方法让利用现有数据安全、高效地训练复杂AI系统成为可能。

[下一步确认点]
1. 该方法在更复杂、更大规模的现实场景（如城市交通模拟、多机器人协同）中的具体效果如何？
2. 算法的计算效率如何？处理海量历史数据会不会太慢？
3. 如何确保用于训练的离线数据集本身具有足够的代表性和质量？
4. 学出的策略在面对训练数据中未出现过的新情况时，其稳健性和安全性如何评估？

content:
arXiv:2603.00376v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: \textit{NeuroHex} is a hexagonal coordinate system designed to support highly efficient world models and reference frames for online adaptive AI systems. Inspired by the hexadirectional firing structure of grid cells in the human brain, NeuroHex adopts a cubic isometric hexagonal coordinate formulation that provides full 60{\deg} rotational symmetry and low-cost translation, rotation and distance computation. We develop a mathematical framework that incorporates ring indexing, quantized angular encoding, and a hierarchical library of foundational, simple, and complex geometric shape primitives. These constructs allow low-overhead point-in-shape tests and spatial matching operations that are expensive in Cartesian coordinate systems. To support realistic settings, the NeuroHex framework can process OpenStreetMap (OSM) data sets using an OSM-to-NeuroHex (\textit{OSM2Hex}) conversion tool. The OSM2Hex spatial abstraction processing pipeline can achieve a reduction of 90-99\% in geometric complexity while maintaining the relevant spatial structure map for navigation. Our initial results, based on actual city and neighborhood scale data sets, demonstrate that NeuroHex offers a highly efficient substrate for building dynamic world models to enable adaptive spatial reasoning in autonomous AI systems with continuous online learning capability.

content:
arXiv:2603.00451v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate and unambiguous guidelines are critical for large language model (LLM) based graders, yet manually crafting these prompts is often sub-optimal as LLMs can misinterpret expert guidelines or lack necessary domain specificity. Consequently, the field has moved toward automated prompt optimization to refine grading guidelines without the burden of manual trial and error. However, existing frameworks typically aggregate independent and unstructured error samples into a single update step, resulting in "rule dilution" where conflicting constraints weaken the model's grading logic. To address these limitations, we introduce Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization (CARO), a novel framework that enhances accuracy and computational efficiency by structurally separating error signals. CARO leverages the confusion matrix to decompose monolithic error signals into distinct modes, allowing for the diagnosis and repair of specific misclassification patterns individually. By synthesizing targeted "fixing patches" for dominant error modes and employing a diversity-aware selection mechanism, the framework prevents guidance conflict and eliminates the need for resource-heavy nested refinement loops. Empirical evaluations on teacher education and STEM datasets demonstrate that CARO significantly outperforms existing SOTA methods. These results suggest that replacing mixed-error aggregation with surgical, mode-specific repair yields robust improvements in automated assessment scalability and precision.
aiSummary: 研究人员提出了一种名为CARO的新框架，用于优化基于大语言模型的自动评分系统。传统方法将所有错误样本混合优化，容易导致规则冲突和逻辑弱化。CARO通过分析混淆矩阵，将错误信号分解为不同模式，并针对主要错误模式生成精准的修复补丁，从而避免指导冲突并提升效率。在教师教育和STEM数据集上的测试表明，其性能显著优于现有方法。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是科学家发明了一种更聪明的方法，来教AI当“阅卷老师”。以前的方法像把所有错题混在一起讲，AI容易听糊涂。新方法能把不同类型的错误分开，一个个重点讲解，让AI“判卷”更准、更快。
aiInterpretation: 一项新研究提出了“混淆感知评分标准优化”（CARO）框架，旨在解决大语言模型在自动评分任务中面临的挑战。传统方法通常将所有独立且非结构化的错误样本汇总到一个更新步骤中，这会导致“规则稀释”，即相互冲突的约束削弱了模型的评分逻辑。CARO框架通过结构性地分离错误信号来应对这一问题。它利用混淆矩阵将整体的错误信号分解为不同的错误模式，从而能够单独诊断和修复特定的误分类模式。该框架为核心错误模式合成有针对性的“修复补丁”，并采用多样性感知的选择机制，既防止了指导冲突，也无需资源密集的嵌套优化循环。在教师教育和STEM数据集上的实证评估显示，CARO显著优于现有的先进方法。 为什么重要：自动评分系统在教育、认证等领域有广泛应用前景，但其准确性和可靠性依赖于清晰、无歧义的评分标准。传统手动或粗放式优化方法效率低下且效果不佳。CARO通过更精细、更具针对性的优化路径，有望提升自动评分的规模化应用能力和精确度，这对于减轻教育工作者负担、实现个性化评估具有重要意义。 影响面： 1. 技术：推动了提示工程和模型优化技术的发展，提供了一种更高效、更精准的错误诊断与修复方法论。 2. 商业：提升了自动化评估产品的性能与可靠性，为教育科技、在线培训、资格认证等市场提供了更强大的工具。 3. 产业链：可能催生更专业的模型优化服务或工具，影响从底层模型提供商到具体应用开发商的产业链分工。 4. 监管/安全：随着自动评分系统更广泛地应用于高风险决策（如升学、职业资格），其准确性与公平性将受到更严格的监管审视，CARO这类提升透明度和可控性的技术有助于满足合规要求。 5. 用户/社会：最终用户（如学生、考生）可能获得更一致、更公平的评估结果；教育者则能从繁重的评分工作中解放出来，更专注于教学。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
科学家开发了一种新方法，让基于大语言模型的AI“自动阅卷系统”变得更聪明、更准确。

[翻成人话]
以前优化AI阅卷规则时，是把所有错误混在一起处理，容易让AI学到矛盾的规则，反而判不准。新方法像一位高明的老师，能精准识别学生常犯的几种典型错误类型，然后针对每种错误进行专项辅导，效果更好。

[为什么重要]
自动评分在教育、考试等领域应用越来越广，但准确性是关键。这种方法能显著提升AI阅卷的精准度和效率，让自动评估更可靠，既能减轻教师负担，也能让评分更公平一致。

[下一步确认点]
这项技术何时能集成到我们常用的在线学习或考试平台中？在实际大规模应用时，对不同学科、不同题型的评分效果是否都同样出色？
fullText: Accurate and unambiguous guidelines are critical for large language model (LLM) based graders, yet manually crafting these prompts is often sub-optimal as LLMs can misinterpret expert guidelines or lack necessary domain specificity. Consequently, the field has moved toward automated prompt optimization to refine grading guidelines without the burden of manual trial and error. However, existing frameworks typically aggregate independent and unstructured error samples into a single update step, resulting in "rule dilution" where conflicting constraints weaken the model's grading logic. To address these limitations, we introduce Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization (CARO), a novel framework that enhances accuracy and computational efficiency by structurally separating error signals. CARO leverages the confusion matrix to decompose monolithic error signals into distinct modes, allowing for the diagnosis and repair of specific misclassification patterns individually. By synthesizing targeted "fixing patches" for dominant error modes and employing a diversity-aware selection mechanism, the framework prevents guidance conflict and eliminates the need for resource-heavy nested refinement loops. Empirical evaluations on teacher education and STEM datasets demonstrate that CARO significantly outperforms existing SOTA methods. These results suggest that replacing mixed-error aggregation with surgical, mode-specific repair yields robust improvements in automated assessment scalability and precision

title: MED-COPILOT: A Medical Assistant Powered by GraphRAG and Similar Patient Case Retrieval

content:
arXiv:2603.00460v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Clinical decision-making requires synthesizing heterogeneous evidence, including patient histories, clinical guidelines, and trajectories of comparable cases. While large language models (LLMs) offer strong reasoning capabilities, they remain prone to hallucinations and struggle to integrate long, structured medical documents. We present MED-COPILOT, an interactive clinical decision-support system designed for clinicians and medical trainees, which combines guideline-grounded GraphRAG retrieval with hybrid semantic-keyword similar-patient retrieval to support transparent and evidence-aware clinical reasoning. The system builds a structured knowledge graph from WHO and NICE guidelines, applies community-level summarization for efficient retrieval, and maintains a 36,000-case similar-patient database derived from SOAP-normalized MIMIC-IV notes and Synthea-generated records.
  We evaluate our framework on clinical note completion and medical question answering, and demonstrate that it consistently outperforms parametric LLM baselines and standard RAG, improving both generation fidelity and clinical reasoning accuracy. The full system is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/Cryo3978/Med_GraphRAG , enabling users to inspect retrieved evidence, visualize token-level similarity contributions, and conduct guided follow-up analysis. Our results demonstrate a practical and interpretable approach to integrating structured guideline knowledge with patient-level analogical evidence for clinical LLMs.
aiSummary: 研究人员推出MED-COPILOT，这是一款专为临床医生和医学生设计的交互式临床决策支持系统。它结合了基于指南的GraphRAG检索和混合语义-关键词相似患者检索，旨在提供透明且基于证据的临床推理支持。该系统从WHO和NICE指南构建知识图谱，并维护一个包含3.6万例相似患者的数据集。评估显示，其在临床记录完成和医学问答任务上，表现优于传统大语言模型和标准检索增强生成方法，提高了生成保真度和临床推理准确性。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，这是一个给医生用的AI助手。它不像普通聊天机器人那样容易‘瞎编’，而是会从权威医疗指南和大量真实病例里找证据，再帮医生分析病情，让诊断建议更靠谱。
aiInterpretation: 临床决策需要综合多种证据，包括患者病史、临床指南和类似病例的轨迹。尽管大语言模型具备强大的推理能力，但它们仍容易产生“幻觉”，且难以整合冗长、结构化的医疗文档。为此，研究人员开发了MED-COPILOT，一个专为临床医生和医学生设计的交互式临床决策支持系统。该系统创新性地结合了基于指南的GraphRAG检索与混合语义-关键词相似患者检索，旨在支持透明且基于证据的临床推理。它从世界卫生组织和英国国家健康与临床优化研究所的指南中构建结构化知识图谱，并维护一个包含3.6万例相似患者的数据集，这些数据来源于标准化的MIMIC-IV病历和Synthea生成的记录。 为什么重要： 在医疗领域，决策的准确性和可靠性至关重要。传统大语言模型在专业医疗场景下存在“幻觉”和整合复杂文档困难的问题，可能导致错误建议。MED-COPILOT通过整合权威指南和真实患者案例，为AI辅助诊断提供了更可靠、可追溯的证据基础，有望提升临床工作的效率和安全性。 影响面： 从技术角度看，该系统展示了GraphRAG与相似病例检索结合的有效性，为医疗AI的检索增强生成技术提供了新范式。在商业层面，此类工具可集成到电子病历或远程诊疗平台，创造新的软件即服务市场。对产业链而言，它推动了从通用AI模型向垂直、专业化医疗辅助工具的演进。在监管与安全方面，系统的透明性和可解释性设计有助于满足医疗AI的合规性要求，降低误诊风险。对于用户与社会，它能为医生提供更精准的决策支持，减轻工作负担，最终可能提升整体医疗服务质量。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
一款名为MED-COPILOT的新型AI医疗助手发布，专为医生和医学生设计。

[翻成人话]
这是一个更聪明的‘AI医生顾问’。它不会自己乱猜，而是懂得先去翻权威的医疗指南书，再从海量真实病历里找出情况最相似的病人案例，结合这两方面证据来帮助医生思考。

[为什么重要]
因为人命关天，医疗AI绝不能出错。这个系统通过提供有据可查的建议，试图解决当前AI在医疗中‘胡言乱语’的核心难题，让AI辅助诊断变得真正可信、可用。

[下一步确认点]
需要关注其在真实医院环境中的长期应用效果，以及如何通过监管审批，确保其安全合规地集成到现有医疗工作流程中。

While large language models (LLMs) offer strong reasoning capabilities, they remain prone to hallucinations and struggle to integrate long, structured medical documents. We present MED-COPILOT, an interactive clinical decision-support system designed for clinicians and medical trainees, which combines guideline-grounded GraphRAG retrieval with hybrid semantic-keyword similar-patient retrieval to support transparent and evidence-aware clinical reasoning.
