title: PRX Part 3 — Training a Text-to-Image Model in 24h!
content: Back to Articles PRX Part 3 — Training a Text-to-Image Model in 24h! Team Article Published March 3, 2026 Upvote 31 +25 David Bertoin Bertoin Follow Photoroom Roman Frigg photoroman Follow Photoroom Jon Almazán jon-almazan Follow Photoroom Introduction Welcome back 👋 In the last two posts ( Part 1 and Part 2 ), we explored a wide range of architectural and training tricks for diffusion models. We tried to evaluate each idea in isolation, measuring throughput, convergence speed, and final image quality, and tried to understand what actually moves the needle. In this post, we want to answer a much more practical question: What happens when we combine all the tricks that worked? Instead of optimizing one dimension at a time, we’ll stack the most promising ingredients together and see how far we can push performance under a strict compute budget. To make things concrete, we’re doing a 24-hour speedrun : 32 H200 ~ $1500 total compute budget (2$/hour/GPU) This is very far from the early diffusion days, where training competitive models could cost millions of dollars. The goal here is to demonstrate how much the field has evolved and how far careful engineering can take you in just a single day of training. This speedrun is not just a fun experiment. It will likely serve as the foundation for our large-scale training recipe going forward. Alongside the results, we’re also open-sourcing our code ( Github link ), which contains: The training code used for this speedrun The experimental framework from the previous blog post So you can reproduce, modify, and extend everything yourself. The Training Recipe Now let’s walk through what went into this 24h run. X-prediction and Training in the Pixel Space We use the x-prediction formulation from Back to Basics: Let Denoising Generative Models Denoise [ Li and He, 2025 ]. As seen in Part 2 , this enables training directly in pixel space and eliminates the need for a VAE altogether . We use a patch size of 32 and use a 256-dimensional bottleneck in the initial token projection layer. This design keeps the sequence length under control, making pixel-space training computationally manageable even at higher resolutions. At 512px, the sequence length is: ( 512 / 32 ) 2 = 256 (512 / 32)^2 = 256 ( 512/32 ) 2 = 256 At 1024px, the sequence length becomes: ( 1024 / 32 ) 2 = 1024 (1024 / 32)^2 = 1024 ( 1024/32 ) 2 = 1024 Instead of following the usual 256px → 512px → 1024px schedule, we start directly at 512px and then fine-tune at 1024px. With controlled token counts and modern hardware, pixel-space training is no longer prohibitive. It is simply a cleaner and more direct formulation. Perceptual Losses One very nice side effect of predicting x 0 x_0 x 0 ​ directly in pixel space is that we can reuse a whole toolbox from classical computer vision. When your model outputs latents, perceptual supervision becomes awkward. You either have to decode back to pixels or define losses in a learned latent space that may or may not align with human perception. Once you predict pixels directly, everything becomes straightforward again. You can plug in perceptual losses exactly as they were originally designed. We take inspiration from the paper PixelGen: Pixel Diffusion Beats Latent Diffusion with Perceptual Loss [ Ma et al. ], where the authors introduce additional perceptual objectives on top of the diffusion loss. They show that adding perceptual signals can noticeably improve convergence speed and final visual quality. For this 24h run, we add two auxiliary losses: LPIPS ([ Zhang et al. ]) A DINO-based perceptual loss (we use DINOv2 [ Oquab et al. ]) The idea is simple: In addition to the standard flow matching objective, we encourage the predicted clean image to match the target image in a perceptual feature space. LPIPS captures low-level perceptual similarity, while DINO features provide a stronger semantic signal. We keep the same overall idea as the paper, but we tweaked a few details. In our experiments, we empirically found that it worked better to: apply the perceptual losses on pooled full images instead of patch-wise features apply them at all noise levels These are small implementation details, but in our setting they consistently gave better results. We used a weight of 0.1 for the LPIPS loss and 0.01 for the DINO perceptual loss, matching the values recommended in the original paper. These losses are lightweight compared to the main transformer forward pass, and in our setup they add only a small overhead while providing a consistent quality boost. Token Routing with TREAD To make each step cheaper, we use token routing with TREAD [ Krause et al., 2025 ]), which randomly selects a fraction of tokens and lets them bypass a contiguous chunk of transformer blocks, then re-injects them later so nothing is dropped. We picked TREAD over SPRINT ( Park et al., 2025 ) mostly for simplicity, and because the extra complexity of SPRINT did not feel worth the fairly small additional compute savings in our setting (sequence length 64 vs. 128 with TREAD at 512px). Following the TREAD recipe, we route 50% of the tokens from the 2nd block to the penultimate block of the transformer. Routed models can look worse under vanilla CFG, especially when undertrained, so we implemented a simple self-guidance scheme inspired by Guiding Token-Sparse Diffusion Models ( Krause et al., 2025 ), which guides using a dense vs. routed conditional prediction instead of relying on an unconditional branch. Representation Alignment with REPA and DINOv3 We used REPA [ Yu et al., 2024 ] for representation alignment. For the teacher, we went with DINOv3 [ Siméoni et al. 2025 ] since it gave the best quality improvements in our previous experiments. Concretely, we apply the alignment loss once,at the 8th transformer block with a loss weight of 0.5 . Since we combine REPA with TREAD routing, we only compute the alignment loss on the non-routed tokens , meaning the tokens that actually go through the blocks where we apply the loss. This keeps the REPA signal consistent and avoids comparing features for tokens that skipped the computation path. Optimizer: Muon We used the Muon optimizer, using the FSDP implementation from muon_fsdp_2 , since it showed a clear improvement over Adam in our previous runs. Muon is only applied to 2D parameters (basically matrices). Everything else (biases, norms, embeddings, etc.) is optimized with Adam, which is why the config has two parameter groups. Group What it applies to Key params we used Muon 2D parameters lr=1e-4 , momentum=0.95 , nesterov=true , ns_steps=5 Adam all non-2D parameters lr=1e-4 , betas=(0.9, 0.95) , eps=1e-8 Training Settings We trained on three publicly available synthetic datasets: Flux generated (1.7M), lehduong/flux_generated FLUX-Reason-6M (6M), LucasFang/FLUX-Reason-6M midjourney-v6-llava (1M), brivangl/midjourney-v6-llava which we re-captioned with Gemini 2.5 Flash to make prompts more consistent and cut down caption noise. The schedule is basically: go fast at 512, then sharpen at 1024: 512px for 100k steps with batch size 1024 1024px for 20k steps with batch size 512 without REPA . We also keep an EMA of the weights for sampling and eval: smoothing = 0.999 update_interval = 10ba ema_start = 0ba Results and Closing Thoughts Below are the evaluation curves we tracked throughout the run and a few sample grids from the final checkpoint: For a one day training run, this is already a pretty solid place to be. The model is not flawless yet (you can still spot some texture glitches, occasional weird anatomy, and it can get a bit shaky on very hard prompts), but it is clearly usable. Prompt following is strong, the overall aesthetic is consistent, and the 1024 stage mostly does what we want: sharpen details without breaking composition. The key takeaway is that we're very close. The remaining issues look more like undertraining artifacts and limited data diversity than signs of a structural flaw in the recipe. The failure modes are consistent with what you’d expect from a model that simply hasn’t seen enough varied data yet. With more compute and broader coverage, this exact setup should continue improving in a fairly predictable way. Zooming out, this speed run also highlights how far diffusion training has come. By combining pixel-space training, efficient routing, representation alignment, and lightweight perceptual guidance, you can now get a meaningful model in about a day on a budget that would have sounded unrealistic not that long ago. What’s next? This 24h run is just a starting point, not the finish line. Next, we will keep pushing the same recipe with a bit more scale and iterate on the dataset mix and captioning. All the code and configs behind this speedrun, as well as the full experimental framework used throughout Part 1 and Part 2 , are available in the PRX repository: https://github.com/Photoroom/PRX . While we don’t redistribute the exact training datasets used in this run, the pipeline is fully configurable and designed to be easily adapted to your own data. You can plug in different datasets, tweak individual components (TREAD, REPA, perceptual losses, Muon, etc.), and run controlled experiments with minimal friction. Our goal is to make this a practical playground for fast diffusion research, and we hope the community will use it to explore, benchmark, and iterate on these techniques in their own setups. If you made it this far, thank you for reading. We would also love to have you join our Discord community, where we share PRX progress and results, and discuss anything diffusion and text-to-image related. Goodbye for now, and stay tuned for the next round of experiments! 🚀 Acknowledgements. This speedrun was inspired by several recent efforts exploring fast and low-cost training of diffusion models. If you're interested in speedrunning text-to-image models, we encourage you to check out the following works: Haridas, A., Shen, T., Yu, J. Nitro-T: Training a Text-to-Image Diffusion Model from Scratch in 1 Day. https://rocm.blogs.amd.com/artificial-intelligence/nitro-t-diffusion/README.html Bhanded, S. Speedrunning ImageNet Diffusion. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.12386 Sehwag, V., Kong, X., Li, J., Spranger, M., Lyu, L. Stretching Each Dollar: Diffusion Training from Scratch on a Micro-Budget. https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.15811 Yeh, S.-Y. Home-made Diffusion Model from Scratch to Hatch. https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.06068 Community andito about 11 hours ago 24 hours is very impressive, but looking at 32 H200 makes it make a bit more sense xD. Super cool that you used Muon! And, are you planning on releasing the re-captioned brivangl/midjourney-v6-llava? 2 replies · ❤️ 1 1 + jon-almazan Article author about 11 hours ago Hey Andres! Thanks! Yeah, definitely not possible on consumer hardware… yet :D That’s also why we added the $1500 tag. Regarding releasing the captions, we hadn’t really thought about that. If you think it would be valuable for the community, we can work on releasing them as well. aiSummary: Photoroom团队在PRX系列第三部分中，成功在24小时内、以约1500美元的成本训练出一个文本生成图像模型。该研究整合了多项高效技术，包括直接在像素空间训练、使用感知损失、TREAD令牌路由、REPA表征对齐以及Muon优化器。结果表明，通过精心设计的工程方案，快速低成本训练高质量扩散模型已成为可能，相关代码已开源。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，就像以前造一辆高性能跑车需要天价和数年时间，但现在通过一系列聪明的设计和现成的好零件组合，只用一天时间和不多的钱就能造出一辆能上路跑得不错的车。这项研究就是找到了在AI绘画领域实现这种“快速平价制造”的秘诀，并且把图纸（代码）公开了。
aiInterpretation: Photoroom团队发布了一项突破性研究，展示了如何在24小时内、使用32块H200 GPU（总成本约1500美元）从头训练一个文本生成图像模型。这项名为“PRX Part 3”的工作并非孤立优化单一指标，而是将一系列经过验证的高效技术进行组合，包括：直接在像素空间进行训练以简化流程；引入LPIPS和DINOv2感知损失来提升图像质量和收敛速度；采用TREAD令牌路由技术降低计算开销；利用REPA与DINOv3进行表征对齐以改善语义理解；以及使用Muon优化器替代部分Adam参数更新。最终模型在512px分辨率训练10万步后，再于1024px分辨率微调2万步，生成的图像在提示跟随、整体美学和细节上已表现出可用性。 为什么重要：这项研究标志着扩散模型训练范式的重要转变。它证明，通过系统性的工程优化和算法创新，将训练成本从早期的数百万美元大幅降低至数千美元级别、并将周期压缩到一天内是可行的。这极大地降低了高质量文本生成图像模型的开发门槛，为更广泛的研究和应用创新铺平了道路。 影响面： 1. 技术：展示了像素空间训练、令牌路由、表征对齐等多种前沿技术组合应用的可行性，为后续模型设计提供了可复现的工程蓝图。 2. 商业：大幅降低了模型训练的资金和时间成本，使得中小团队甚至个人研究者进行模型迭代和创新成为可能，可能催生更多商业化应用。 3. 产业链：开源全套代码和实验框架，有助于推动整个AI开源生态的发展，加速社区协作和技术迭代。 4. 用户/社会：更低的训练成本最终可能使更强大、更易获取的图像生成工具得以普及，影响内容创作、设计、教育等多个社会领域。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
AI公司Photoroom宣布，他们成功在24小时内、花费约1500美元就训练出了一个能从文字描述生成图片的AI模型。

[翻成人话]
以前训练一个能“文生图”的AI，动不动就要烧掉几百万美元和好几个月。现在，通过把好几项省时省力的新技术像搭积木一样组合起来，他们只用了一天时间和相当于一部高端手机的钱就搞定了。

[为什么重要]
这就像把AI模型的“出厂成本”和“生产时间”打了个巨大的折扣。以后，更多的研究团队和小公司都有能力去开发和改进自己的AI绘画模型了，可能会让好用的AI绘画工具变得更多、更便宜。

[下一步确认点]
1. 这个一天练成的模型，实际画图效果到底有多好？和花几个月训练的模型差距大吗？
2. 他们公开的“秘诀”（开源代码），其他人能顺利复现吗？
3. 成本还能不能再降？未来有没有可能在个人电脑上实现类似训练？

title: GPT-5.3 Instant System Card
aiSummary: OpenAI发布了GPT-5.3 Instant的系统卡，详细介绍了其安全与能力评估框架。该文档旨在提高模型透明度，并概述了在部署前为减轻潜在风险所采取的缓解措施。这标志着AI公司在模型治理和负责任发布方面迈出了重要一步。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是OpenAI给他们的新AI模型做了一份详细的‘体检报告’，告诉大家这个模型有多厉害，同时也说明了他们为了防止它出问题做了哪些准备。
aiInterpretation: OpenAI近期发布了针对其最新模型GPT-5.3 Instant的系统卡。这份文件详细阐述了该模型在部署前所经历的安全评估、能力测试以及为应对潜在风险（如偏见、错误信息生成或滥用）而设计的缓解策略。系统卡的核心目标是提升模型开发的透明度，并建立一套标准化的安全评估流程。 为什么重要：随着AI模型能力日益强大，其潜在的社会影响和风险也备受关注。发布系统卡是行业向更负责任、更透明的AI开发与部署模式转变的关键举措。它不仅是向用户和监管机构展示安全承诺的方式，也为整个行业设定了在模型发布前进行系统性风险评估的基准。 影响面： - **技术**：推动了AI模型安全评估方法论的发展，为能力与风险的系统性测试提供了框架。 - **商业**：通过展示对安全的重视，有助于建立用户信任，可能影响企业客户（尤其是对合规要求高的行业）的采用决策。 - **监管/安全**：为监管机构提供了评估AI系统安全性的参考依据，可能影响未来AI安全法规和标准的制定。 - **用户/社会**：增强了公众对AI系统安全性的了解，有助于管理社会对AI技术的期望，并促进关于AI伦理的公共讨论。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
OpenAI发布了关于其最新AI模型GPT-5.3 Instant的一份重要安全说明文件，叫做“系统卡”。

[翻成人话]
就像给一个强大的新工具附上一份详细的使用说明书和安全须知，告诉大家它有什么本事，以及厂家为了防止它被误用或出问题，提前做了哪些防护措施。

[为什么重要]
因为AI越来越强大，提前公开、透明地说明它的能力和潜在风险，是赢得大家信任、确保技术被负责任使用的关键一步。这也能推动整个行业更注重安全。

[下一步确认点]
接下来可以关注其他AI公司是否会跟进发布类似的报告，以及监管机构是否会参考这类文件来制定新的规则。

title: GPT-5.3 Instant: Smoother, more useful everyday conversations
aiSummary: OpenAI发布GPT-5.3 Instant模型，专注于提升日常对话的流畅度与实用性。新模型在响应速度、上下文理解及多轮对话连贯性上均有显著改进，旨在让AI助手更像一个自然、有用的对话伙伴。该模型现已通过API向开发者开放，并计划逐步集成至ChatGPT等产品中。
aiExplanation: 简单说，OpenAI推出了一个新版聊天AI，它不一定更“聪明”，但肯定更“好聊”。它反应更快，更能理解你的言外之意，聊天时不容易前言不搭后语，目标是成为你更顺手的日常助手。
aiInterpretation: OpenAI近日发布了其最新对话模型GPT-5.3 Instant。该模型并非追求参数规模的巨大突破，而是将研发重点放在了优化日常对话体验上，致力于让AI对话更平滑、更有用。新模型在多个方面进行了针对性改进，包括大幅提升响应速度、增强对复杂上下文和细微意图的理解能力，以及显著改善多轮对话的连贯性与逻辑性。其目标是让用户感觉像是在与一个反应迅速、理解力强且始终在线的伙伴交谈。 为什么重要：这项进展标志着AI技术发展从单纯追求“更大更强”的模型，转向更精细地打磨用户体验和实际应用效果。它直接回应了用户对现有AI助手在长对话中容易“跑偏”或“遗忘”上下文的核心痛点，是AI实用化进程中的关键一步。 影响面： - 技术：展示了在现有模型架构下，通过针对性优化而非单纯扩大规模，也能显著提升特定场景（如日常对话）的性能。 - 商业：为开发者提供了更高效、更易集成的对话AI工具，可能催生更多注重交互体验的应用和服务。 - 用户/社会：普通用户将获得更自然、更可靠的AI对话体验，降低使用门槛，使AI助手更无缝地融入日常生活与工作。 - 监管/安全：更流畅的对话能力可能带来更难以察觉的误导或滥用风险，对内容安全与真实性验证提出了更高要求。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
OpenAI发布了一个新的聊天AI模型，叫GPT-5.3 Instant。

[翻成人话]
这个新模型不追求变得无比庞大复杂，而是专心解决“不好聊”的问题。它让AI跟你对话时，反应更快，更懂你的弦外之音，聊天过程更连贯，不那么容易跑题或犯傻。

[为什么重要]
这代表AI发展的重点变了，从一味追求“更大”转向精心打磨“好用”。它直接瞄准了我们现在用聊天AI时最头疼的问题——聊着聊着它就忘了前面在说什么。这是AI真正变得实用的重要标志。

[下一步确认点]
接下来需要关注：这个新模型具体会用在ChatGPT的哪些功能上？它对普通用户免费开放吗？以及，随着对话更“以假乱真”，平台会如何防止它被用于诈骗或传播虚假信息？

title: Join or host a GitHub Copilot Dev Days event near you
summary:
GitHub Copilot Dev Days is a global series of hands-on, in-person, community-led events designed to help developers explore real-world, AI-assisted coding.

The post Join or host a GitHub Copilot Dev Days event near you appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
content: Home / AI & ML / GitHub Copilot Join or host a GitHub Copilot Dev Days event near you GitHub Copilot Dev Days is a global series of hands-on, in-person, community-led events designed to help developers explore real-world, AI-assisted coding. Kathleen Ann Savage · @katiesavage March 3, 2026 | 3 minutes Share: The way we build software is changing fast. AI is no longer a “someday” tool. It’s reshaping how we plan, write, review, and ship code right now. As products evolve faster than ever, developers are expected to keep up just as quickly. That’s why GitHub Copilot Dev Days exists: for developers to level up together on how they can use AI-assisted coding today. GitHub Copilot Dev Days is a global series of hands-on, in-person, community-led events designed to help developers explore real-world AI-assisted coding with GitHub Copilot . Join us for the knowledge, stay for the great food, good vibes, and plenty of fun along the way. Find an event near you and register today. Who is GitHub Copilot Dev Days for? Anyone and everyone who is looking to improve their development workflow and learn something new! We have events run by and for folks from professional developers to students. Sessions cover various levels and programming backgrounds. If it’s your first time trying out AI-assisted development, this event will introduce you to the tools and best practices to succeed from day one. If you’re more advanced, we’re excited to show you the latest tips and tricks to ensure you’re fully up to date. What to expect from a GitHub Copilot Dev Day Each event will feature live demos, practical sessions, and interactive workshops with high-quality training content. We will focus on real workflows you can use right away, whether you’re already using Copilot daily or just getting started. Your hosts are development experts: GitHub Stars, Microsoft MVPs, GitHub Campus Experts, Microsoft Student Ambassadors, GitHub and Microsoft employees, to name a few. We will have training materials covering the GitHub Copilot CLI, Cloud Agent, GitHub Copilot in VS Code, Visual Studio, and other editors , and more! Different events will focus on different topics, so be sure to review the registration page beforehand. The specific event details will vary, as each community event organizer might tweak the event to fit the interests of their local developer community. Here is a sample agenda: Introductory session : 30-45 minutes on GitHub Copilot. Local community session : 30-45 minutes by a local developer or community leader on relevant topics. Hands-on workshop : 1 hour of coding and practical exercises. All events are an opportunity to connect with your local developer community, learn something new, and enjoy some snacks and swag! Events begin in March Events are now live in cities around the world starting in March. Spots are limited and dates are approaching—now’s the time to grab a seat. Want to bring GitHub Copilot Dev Days to your user group? Fill out our form . Find a GitHub Copilot Dev Days event near you and register today > Tags: GitHub Copilot Written by Kathleen Ann Savage @katiesavage Technical Program Manager Community Advocate GitHub Copilot More on GitHub Copilot From idea to pull request: A practical guide to building with GitHub Copilot CLI A hands-on guide to using GitHub Copilot CLI to move from intent to reviewable changes, and how that work flows naturally into your IDE and GitHub. Ari LiVigni What's new with GitHub Copilot coding agent GitHub Copilot coding agent now includes a model picker, self-review, built-in security scanning, custom agents, and CLI handoff. Here’s what’s new and how to use it. Andrea Griffiths Related posts AI & ML From idea to pull request: A practical guide to building with GitHub Copilot CLI A hands-on guide to using GitHub Copilot CLI to move from intent to reviewable changes, and how that work flows naturally into your IDE and GitHub. AI & ML What’s new with GitHub Copilot coding agent GitHub Copilot coding agent now includes a model picker, self-review, built-in security scanning, custom agents, and CLI handoff. Here’s what’s new and how to use it. AI & ML Multi-agent workflows often fail. Here’s how to engineer ones that don’t. Most multi-agent workflow failures come down to missing structure, not model capability. Learn the three engineering patterns that make agent systems reliable. Explore more from GitHub Docs Everything you need to master GitHub, all in one place. Go to Docs GitHub Build what’s next on GitHub, the place for anyone from anywhere to build anything. Start building Customer stories Meet the companies and engineering teams that build with GitHub. Learn more The GitHub Podcast Catch up on the GitHub podcast, a show dedicated to the topics, trends, stories and culture in and around the open source developer community on GitHub. See the GitHub Privacy Statement for more details. Subscribe
aiSummary: GitHub宣布启动全球线下活动系列“GitHub Copilot Dev Days”，旨在帮助开发者通过实践掌握AI辅助编程技能。活动由社区主导，包含现场演示、实践工作坊和专家指导，覆盖从初学者到高级开发者的不同需求。活动将于三月在全球多个城市启动，名额有限。
aiExplanation: 简单说，GitHub在全球组织线下聚会，教程序员怎么用AI工具（Copilot）来更聪明、更快地写代码。有吃有喝还能认识同行，从新手到高手都能参加。
aiInterpretation: GitHub正式推出名为“GitHub Copilot Dev Days”的全球系列线下活动。该活动由社区主导，以实践操作为核心，旨在帮助开发者探索并掌握如何在实际工作中使用GitHub Copilot进行AI辅助编程。活动内容涵盖现场演示、实践工作坊和互动环节，由GitHub Stars、微软MVP等专家主持，培训材料涉及Copilot CLI、Cloud Agent及在VS Code等编辑器中的应用。活动将于三月起在全球多个城市陆续展开。 为什么重要：AI辅助编程正迅速改变软件开发方式，成为开发者提升效率的关键工具。此类线下活动提供了系统学习和实践的平台，能加速开发者对新工具的适应，并促进最佳实践的社区共享。 影响面： 1. 技术：普及AI辅助编程工具的具体应用，降低开发者学习门槛，推动相关技术采纳。 2. 商业：通过培育开发者生态，增强GitHub Copilot产品的用户粘性和市场渗透，为微软及GitHub创造商业机会。 3. 产业链：活动由社区组织者因地制宜调整，有助于在全球范围内形成本地化的AI编程支持网络，影响软件开发服务与培训产业链。 4. 用户/社会：直接惠及从学生到专业开发者的广泛群体，通过面对面交流增强社区联结，并可能影响未来软件开发人才的技能结构。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
GitHub宣布启动全球线下活动“GitHub Copilot Dev Days”，教开发者用AI写代码。

[翻成人话]
GitHub要在全世界办线下培训班，手把手教程序员怎么用他们家的AI编程助手Copilot。活动有吃有玩，还能认识本地同行。

[为什么重要]
因为AI辅助编程已经是现在进行时，能大幅提升开发效率。这类活动能让大家快速上手，并交流经验。

[下一步确认点]
活动三月开始，全球多个城市都有。想参加或想自己办一场，得赶紧去官网查位置或填表申请。

title: NeuroHex: Highly-Efficient Hex Coordinate System for Creating World Models to Enable Adaptive AI
summary:

content:
arXiv:2603.00376v2 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: NeuroHex is a hexagonal coordinate system designed to support highly efficient world models and reference frames for online adaptive AI systems. Inspired by the hexadirectional firing structure of grid cells in the human brain, NeuroHex adopts a cubic isometric hexagonal coordinate formulation that provides full 60{\deg} rotational symmetry and low-cost translation, rotation and distance computation. We develop a mathematical framework that incorporates ring indexing, quantized angular encoding, and a hierarchical library of foundational, simple, and complex geometric shape primitives. These constructs allow low-overhead point-in-shape tests and spatial matching operations that are expensive in Cartesian coordinate systems. To support realistic settings, the NeuroHex framework can process OpenStreetMap (OSM) data sets using an OSM-to-NeuroHex (OSM2Hex) conversion tool. The OSM2Hex spatial abstraction processing pipeline can achieve a reduction of 90-99% in geometric complexity while maintaining the relevant spatial structure map for navigation. Our initial results, based on actual city and neighborhood scale data sets, demonstrate that NeuroHex offers a highly efficient substrate for building dynamic world models to enable adaptive spatial reasoning in autonomous AI systems with continuous online learning capability.
aiSummary: 研究人员开发出名为NeuroHex的新型六边形坐标系统，灵感源自大脑网格细胞的六向放电结构。该系统具备60度完全旋转对称性，能大幅降低空间转换与距离计算成本。通过配套工具，可将现实地图数据（如OpenStreetMap）的几何复杂度降低90%-99%，为具备持续在线学习能力的自主AI系统构建动态世界模型提供高效基础。
aiInterpretation: NeuroHex是一种新型六边形坐标系统，专为支持在线自适应AI系统的高效世界模型和参考系而设计。其核心灵感来源于人类大脑网格细胞的六向放电结构，采用立方等距六边形坐标公式，实现了完全的60度旋转对称性，并显著降低了平移、旋转和距离计算的计算成本。研究团队开发了一套数学框架，包含环索引、量化角度编码以及基础、简单和复杂几何形状图元的层次化库。这些构造使得在笛卡尔坐标系中成本高昂的“点是否在形状内”测试和空间匹配操作变得开销极低。为支持现实场景，NeuroHex框架可通过OSM2Hex转换工具处理OpenStreetMap（OSM）数据集，其空间抽象处理流程能在保持导航所需相关空间结构的同时，将几何复杂度降低90%至99%。基于实际城市和街区数据集的初步结果表明，NeuroHex为构建动态世界模型提供了高效基础，有望赋能具备持续在线学习能力的自主AI系统进行自适应空间推理。 为什么重要： 这项技术的重要性在于，它从神经科学中汲取灵感，为AI系统理解和交互复杂、动态的现实世界提供了一种更接近生物智能基础原理的数学与计算框架。传统笛卡尔坐标系在处理旋转、距离和空间关系时存在计算效率瓶颈，而NeuroHex通过其固有的对称性和高效运算，有望成为下一代自主AI（如自动驾驶汽车、机器人）构建实时、精确环境模型的关键基础设施。 影响面： 技术层面：为AI空间推理引入了新的高效计算范式，可能推动导航、规划算法的革新。 商业层面：可降低自动驾驶、机器人、AR/VR等领域对高算力硬件的依赖，提升产品能效与成本效益。 产业链层面：可能催生新的地图数据预处理、AI模型开发工具链及配套服务。 监管/安全层面：高效精确的世界模型对确保自动驾驶等系统的安全决策至关重要，但也需建立对新坐标系统输出结果的验证标准。 用户/社会层面：最终可能使更智能、适应性更强的自主系统更早惠及日常生活，提升交通、物流等服务的效率与安全性。
aiExplanation: 简单说，科学家模仿大脑认路的方式，发明了一种新的“六边形地图格”。用它来教AI认识周围环境，算得更快、更省力，就像给AI装上了更高效的大脑GPS，未来能让机器人、自动驾驶汽车变得更聪明。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
科学家受大脑启发，发明了一种名为NeuroHex的新型“六边形地图”系统，用于帮助AI更高效地理解和适应真实世界。

[翻成人话]
给AI用的导航系统升级了！模仿人脑认路细胞的排列方式，改用六边形网格来画地图，让AI算位置、转方向又快又省“脑力”。

[为什么重要]
现在的AI（比如自动驾驶汽车）用传统方法理解复杂环境很吃力，耗电又慢。这个新系统能极大提升效率，是让AI变得更灵活、更接近人类智能的关键一步。

[下一步确认点]
这项技术何时能从实验室测试走向实际应用？它能否与现有自动驾驶或机器人系统顺利整合？大规模使用时，其稳定性和准确性如何保证？
fullText: NeuroHex is a hexagonal coordinate system designed to support highly efficient world models and reference frames for online adaptive AI systems. Inspired by the hexadirectional firing structure of grid cells in the human brain, NeuroHex adopts a cubic isometric hexagonal coordinate formulation that provides full 60° rotational symmetry and low-cost translation, rotation and distance computation. We develop a mathematical framework that incorporates ring indexing, quantized angular encoding, and a hierarchical library of foundational, simple, and complex geometric shape primitives. These constructs allow low-overhead point-in-shape tests and spatial matching operations that are expensive in Cartesian coordinate systems. To support realistic settings, the NeuroHex framework can process OpenStreetMap (OSM) data sets using an OSM-to-NeuroHex (OSM2Hex) conversion tool. The OSM2Hex spatial abstraction processing pipeline can achieve a reduction of 90-99% in geometric complexity while maintaining the relevant spatial structure map for navigation. Our initial results, based on actual city and neighborhood scale data sets, demonstrate that NeuroHex offers a highly efficient substrate for building dynamic world models to enable adaptive spatial reasoning in autonomous AI systems with continuous online learning capability

title: Bridging the operational AI gap
summary: The transformational potential of AI is already well established. Enterprise use cases are building momentum and organizations are transitioning from pilot projects to AI in production. Companies are no longer just talking about AI; they are redirecting budgets and resources to make it happen. Many are already experimenting with agentic AI, which promises new levels…
content: Sponsored Artificial intelligence Bridging the operational AI gap Enterprise-wide integration is being leveraged to extend today’s process automations into tomorrow’s agentic workflows. By MIT Technology Review Insights archive page March 4, 2026 In partnership with Celigo The transformational potential of AI is already well established. Enterprise use cases are building momentum and organizations are transitioning from pilot projects to AI in production. Companies are no longer just talking about AI; they are redirecting budgets and resources to make it happen. Many are already experimenting with agentic AI, which promises new levels of automation. Yet, the road to full operational success is still uncertain for many. And, while AI experimentation is everywhere, enterprise-wide adoption remains elusive. Without integrated data and systems, stable automated workflows, and governance models, AI initiatives can get stuck in pilots and struggle to move into production. The rise of agentic AI and increasing model autonomy make a holistic approach to integrating data, applications, and systems more important than ever. Without it, enterprise AI initiatives may fail. Gartner predicts over 40% of agentic AI projects will be cancelled by 2027 due to cost, inaccuracy, and governance challenges. The real issue is not the AI itself, but the missing operational foundation. DOWNLOAD THE REPORT To understand how organizations are structuring their AI operations and how they are deploying successful AI projects, MIT Technology Review Insights surveyed 500 senior IT leaders at mid- to large-size companies in the US, all of which are pursuing AI in some way. The results of the survey, along with a series of expert interviews, all conducted in December 2025, show that a strong integration foundation aligns with more advanced AI implementations, conducive to enterprise-wide initiatives. As AI technologies and applications evolve and proliferate, an integration platform can help organizations avoid duplication and silos, and have clear oversight as they navigate the growing autonomy of workflows. Key findings from the report include the following: Some organizations are making progress with AI. In recent years, study after study has exposed a lack of tangible AI success. Yet, our research finds three in four (76%) surveyed companies have at least one department with an AI workflow fully in production. AI succeeds most frequently with well-defined, established processes. Nearly half (43%) of organizations are finding success with AI implementations applied to well-defined and automated processes. A quarter are succeeding with new processes. And one-third (32%) are applying AI to various processes. Two-thirds of organizations lack dedicated AI teams. Only one in three (34%) organizations have a team specifically for maintaining AI workflows. One in five (21%) say central IT is responsible for ongoing AI maintenance, and 25% say the responsibility lies with departmental operations. For 19% of organizations, the responsibility is spread out. Enterprise-wide integration platforms lead to more robust implementation of AI. Companies with enterprise-wide integration platforms are five times more likely to use more diverse data sources in AI workflows. Six in 10 (59%) employ five or more data sources, compared to only 11% of organizations using integration for specific workflows, or 0% of those not using an integration platform. Organizations using integration platforms also have more multi-departmental implementation of AI, more autonomy in AI workflows, and more confidence in assigning autonomy in the future. Download the report. This content was produced by Insights, the custom content arm of MIT Technology Review. It was not written by MIT Technology Review’s editorial staff. It was researched, designed, and written by human writers, editors, analysts, and illustrators. This includes the writing of surveys and collection of data for surveys. AI tools that may have been used were limited to secondary production processes that passed thorough human review. By Michelle Kim archive page Moltbook was peak AI theater The viral social network for bots reveals more about our own current mania for AI as it does about the future of agents. aiSummary: 《麻省理工科技评论》洞察报告指出，企业AI应用正从试点转向生产，但全面运营成功仍面临挑战。报告发现，76%的受访公司至少有一个部门拥有完全投入生产的AI工作流。然而，缺乏集成数据、系统和治理模型是主要障碍。高德纳预测，到2027年，超过40%的智能体AI项目可能因成本、准确性和治理问题被取消。报告强调，企业级集成平台是成功的关键，能显著提升数据利用率和跨部门实施能力。
aiInterpretation: 根据《麻省理工科技评论》洞察部门与Celigo合作发布的报告，企业人工智能应用正在加速从试点项目转向生产部署，但实现全面运营成功仍面临显著障碍。报告基于2025年12月对500名美国中大型企业高级IT领导者的调查。关键发现包括：76%的受访公司至少有一个部门拥有完全投入生产的AI工作流；43%的组织在应用于定义明确且已自动化的流程时最易成功；但三分之二的组织缺乏专门的AI团队。报告特别指出，企业级集成平台是推动AI成功的关键因素，使用此类平台的公司在其AI工作流中使用多样化数据源的可能性高出五倍。 为什么重要：AI的变革潜力已获公认，但许多企业的AI计划因缺乏运营基础而停滞在试点阶段，无法转化为实际生产力。报告揭示了从“谈论AI”到“运营AI”之间的关键缺口，即集成的数据和系统、稳定的自动化工作流以及治理模型。这直接关系到企业能否抓住AI带来的效率提升和自动化新层级（如智能体AI）的机遇。 影响面： 1. 技术：智能体AI和模型自主性的兴起，使得对数据、应用和系统进行整体集成的技术方法变得比以往任何时候都更重要。缺乏集成将导致项目失败。 2. 商业：企业正在重新调整预算和资源以推动AI落地。成功的AI实施能带来流程自动化升级和新的工作模式，直接影响企业效率和竞争力。 3. 产业链：集成平台提供商（如报告合作伙伴Celigo）的角色凸显，它们帮助企业连接不同系统和数据源，成为AI成功落地产业链上的关键一环。 4. 监管/安全：治理挑战是导致AI项目被取消的主要原因之一。缺乏清晰的治理模型和 oversight（监督）会引发准确性、成本和合规性风险。 5. 用户/社会：在企业内部，AI工作流的部署和维护责任归属分散（19%的组织责任分散），这影响了AI工具的稳定性和用户体验，也反映了组织适应新技术的社会性调整过程。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是很多公司都在试人工智能，但真正能让AI像水电一样在公司里顺畅运行、干实活的还不多。主要卡在数据不通、系统不连、管理规则不清。报告说，用好一个能打通全公司数据的“连接器”平台，是成功的关键。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
《麻省理工科技评论》发布企业AI应用调查报告，指出企业AI正从试验走向实用，但成功的关键在于打好数据和系统集成的基础。

[翻成人话]
很多公司都在搞人工智能，但真能让AI干活、出效果的还不多。问题主要出在各部门数据不通、软件系统各管各的，导致AI模型“学”不好也“用”不顺。报告发现，用好一个能打通全公司数据的“连接平台”是成功秘诀。

[为什么重要]
这关系到企业投下去的真金白银能不能换来实际效率。如果基础没打好，超过40%的AI项目可能因为太烧钱、效果差或管理混乱而在几年内被砍掉。打好集成基础，才能让AI从“玩具”变成“工具”。

[下一步确认点]
企业需要评估自身的数据和系统集成程度，考虑投资企业级集成平台，并建立明确的AI治理团队和规则，以确保AI项目能稳定运行并创造价值。

title: The Download: Earth’s rumblings, and AI for strikes on Iran
summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Listen to Earth’s rumbling, secret soundtrack The boom of a calving glacier. The crackling rumble of a wildfire. The roar of a surging storm front. They’re the noises of the living…
content: The Download The Download: Earth’s rumblings, and AI for strikes on Iran Plus: How Anthropic’s AI is being used for US strikes on Iran. By Charlotte Jee archive page March 4, 2026 This is today's edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what's going on in the world of technology. Listen to Earth’s rumbling, secret soundtrack The boom of a calving glacier. The crackling rumble of a wildfire. The roar of a surging storm front. They’re the noises of the living Earth, but as loud as all these things are, they emit even more acoustic energy below the threshold of human hearing, at frequencies of 20 hertz or lower. These “infrasounds” have such long wavelengths that they can travel around the globe as churning emanations of distant events. But humans have never been able to hear them. Until now. Read our story and check the sounds out for yourself . —Monique Brouillette This story is from the latest March/April issue of our print magazine, all about crime. Subscribe today to get full access. You’ll also receive an in-depth digital AI report and an exclusive e-book on how to understand AI’s reckoning. MIT Technology Review Narrated: The curious case of the disappearing Lamborghinis A new wave of theft is rocking the luxury car industry—mixing high tech with old-school chop-shop techniques to snag vehicles while they’re in transport. It’s remained under the radar, even as it’s rocked the industry over the past two years. MIT Technology Review identified more than a dozen cases involving high-end vehicles, obtained court records, and spoke to law enforcement, brokers, drivers, and victims in multiple states to reveal how transport fraud is wreaking havoc across the country. This is our latest story to be turned into a MIT Technology Review Narrated podcast, which we’re publishing each week on Spotify and Apple Podcasts . Just navigate to MIT Technology Review Narrated on either platform, and follow us to get all our new content as it’s released. The must-reads I’ve combed the internet to find you today’s most fun/important/scary/fascinating stories about technology. 1 How Anthropic’s AI tool Claude is being used for US strikes on Iran It’s helping to identify targets and prioritize them—for now. ( WP $) + We should all be alarmed by the White House turning on Anthropic. ( The Atlantic $) + OpenAI is pursuing a contract with NATO. ( Reuters ) 2 Iran’s Shahed drones give it a major advantage They’re cheap and easy to manufacture, but very expensive to intercept. ( CNBC ) + The US is manufacturing copies of the drone to use against Iran. ( New Scientist $) + Israel’s plot to kill Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was years in the making. ( FT $) 3 Data center politics are getting an early test in North Carolina One of the candidates is calling for a 10-year national moratorium on building them. ( The Guardian ) + But it’s not just data centers that are driving people’s electricity bills up. ( Inside Climate News ) + Data centers are amazing. Everyone hates them. ( MIT Technology Review ) + Never mind space—why not just build them into floating offshore wind turbines? ( IEEE Spectrum ) 4 LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users At a speed and scale far beyond what even skilled human investigators can manage. ( Ars Technica ) + It’s also very easy to persuade them to fabricate scientific papers. ( Nature $) 5 TikTok has ruled out end-to-end encryption, citing user safety It’s a stance that sets it apart from almost all rival social media services. ( BBC ) + The strategy will please parents, police—and hackers. ( Cybernews ) + TikTok is experiencing Oracle-related server issues, again. ( Gizmodo ) 6 Why is SpaceX going public? One thing seems certain: it’s not for the reasons Musk’s claiming. ( The Verge $) + Two companies have just unveiled plans to build lunar harvesters . ( Ars Technica ) 7 NASA’s scheduled its next attempt to launch the Artemis II moon rocket On April Fool’s Day, of all days. Good luck! ( Space ) 8 What it’s like to live with a brain implant for years 🧠 For 65-year-old Rodney Gorham, who can no longer walk, talk, or move his hands, it’s been a real lifeline. ( Wired $) + This patient’s Neuralink brain implant is getting a boost from generative AI. ( MIT Technology Review ) 9 Pokémon Pokopia is getting rave reviews It apparently mixes Animal Crossing and Stardew Valley, with a hint of Minecraft-style building. ( BBC ) 10. Hollywood is scouring YouTube for its next horror hits 🔪 Movie studios want to bring the threat from the platform in-house. ( The New Yorker $) + One YouTuber’s self-financed horror flick opened at 4,000 theatres. ( Variety ) Quote of the day “I think it just looked opportunistic and sloppy.” —OpenAI CEO Sam Altman comments on X about his decision to rush in to work with the US Department of War after its talks with Anthropic fell apart. One More Thing Crypto millionaires are pouring money into Central America to build their own cities El Salvador’s Conchagua Volcano, home to a lush ecotourism retreat amid its sun-dappled forest, is set to host a glittering new Bitcoin City, according to the country’s president. While some politicians and residents believe in crypto’s potential to jump-start the economy, others see history repeating itself. They also question who these projects are really for, and whether the countries serving as test beds will truly benefit. Read the full story . —Laurie Clarke We can still have nice things A place for comfort, fun and distraction to brighten up your day. (Got any ideas? Drop me a line or skeet 'em at me .) + Art is everywhere in Los Angeles : you just need to know what you’re looking for. + Survivor has been running for 50 seasons. How is that even possible ?! + MP3 players are cool again. I don’t make the rules. + Be careful out there—you never know when you’re going to come across a Homer Simpson AI cover song . aiSummary: 美国军方正使用Anthropic公司的人工智能工具Claude来协助识别和优先打击伊朗目标，这标志着AI在军事决策中的角色日益关键。与此同时，地球的低频声音首次被转化为人类可听见的音频，揭示了冰川崩解、野火等自然现象的隐秘声景。这两项进展分别指向了AI技术应用的伦理边界与人类感知自然的全新维度。
aiInterpretation: 本期科技简报聚焦两项重要进展：人工智能在军事领域的深度应用，以及人类首次“听见”地球的低频声音。 为什么重要： 首先，美国军方使用Anthropic公司的AI工具Claude辅助识别和优先排序对伊朗的打击目标，这标志着生成式AI正从民用领域快速渗透至军事决策核心。此类应用引发了关于AI自主性、责任归属及伦理风险的广泛担忧。其次，科学家成功将地球产生的、频率低于20赫兹的“次声”转化为人类可听见的声音，使我们得以感知冰川崩裂、野火蔓延等自然事件的隐秘声景，这不仅是科学探索的突破，也提供了监测环境变化的新工具。 影响面： 从技术层面看，军事AI的应用推动了目标识别算法的精准化，而次声转化技术则拓展了人类的数据感知与处理能力。商业上，军事AI合同可能重塑国防科技行业的竞争格局，催生新的供应链需求。产业链方面，涉及从AI模型训练、数据标注到专用硬件（如用于拦截伊朗无人机的仿制品生产）的多个环节。监管与安全挑战尤为突出，包括对致命性自主武器系统的国际规范缺位，以及AI生成虚假科学论文、大型语言模型去匿名化用户所带来的新型安全威胁。对社会与用户而言，军事AI的“黑箱”决策可能削弱公众问责，而感知地球声音则能提升公众的环保意识与科学素养。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，这期新闻讲了两件大事：一是美国开始用高级AI帮忙选择军事打击目标，这很厉害但也让人担心；二是科学家第一次让我们‘听到’了地球本身发出的、以前人耳听不见的低沉声音，比如冰山融化时的巨响。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
美国军方使用AI工具辅助打击伊朗目标，同时科学家首次将地球的‘次声’转化为可听见的音频。

[翻成人话]
打仗用上了AI帮手来挑目标，另外我们终于能‘听见’地球自己发出的低沉声音了。

[为什么重要]
AI用于军事决策可能改变战争形态并引发伦理问题，而‘听见’地球则为我们理解自然环境打开了新窗口。

[下一步确认点]
需要关注国际社会对军事AI的监管讨论，以及这项声音转化技术将如何具体应用于地震或气候监测。

title: Extending single-minus amplitudes to gravitons
summary: A new preprint extends single-minus amplitudes to gravitons, with GPT-5.2 Pro helping derive and verify nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity.
content: A new preprint extends single-minus amplitudes to gravitons, with GPT-5.2 Pro helping derive and verify nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity
fullText: A new preprint extends single-minus amplitudes to gravitons, with GPT-5.2 Pro helping derive and verify nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity
aiSummary: 一篇新预印本论文将单负振幅理论扩展至引力子领域，并借助GPT-5.2 Pro辅助推导与验证了量子引力中非零的引力子树图振幅。这一进展为理解引力在量子层面的行为提供了新的计算工具和理论线索，可能推动量子引力研究向前迈进。
aiInterpretation: 一篇新发布的预印本论文取得了理论物理领域的重要进展，成功将单负振幅理论扩展应用于引力子。研究团队在人工智能模型GPT-5.2 Pro的辅助下，推导并验证了量子引力理论中非零的引力子树图振幅。 为什么重要： 这项工作之所以关键，是因为它触及了现代物理学最核心的难题之一——如何将引力纳入量子力学的框架，即量子引力问题。引力子作为传递引力的假想基本粒子，其振幅的计算是构建量子引力理论的关键步骤。此前，相关计算极为复杂且困难。本研究不仅提供了新的计算结果，更重要的是展示了先进人工智能工具在辅助前沿理论推导与验证方面的巨大潜力，可能为这一长期停滞的领域开辟新的研究范式。 影响面： 从技术层面看，该研究开发了计算引力子振幅的新方法，提升了理论物理的计算能力。在商业层面，它虽不直接产生产品，但其所依赖和验证的AI工具（如GPT-5.2 Pro）的能力得到彰显，可能吸引更多科技投资进入基础科研辅助AI领域。对产业链而言，基础物理理论的突破长期来看是颠覆性技术（如未来能源、航天）的源头，但影响非常远期。在监管/安全维度，对物质基本结构和引力的深入理解，长远可能涉及对时空本质的认知，但目前暂无直接关联。对用户/社会的影响是间接的，它满足了人类对宇宙根本规律的好奇心，并展示了AI与科学深度结合的可能前景，激励更多跨学科探索。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，科学家们用高级人工智能帮忙，在理论上算出了引力这种基本力在微观世界可能的表现形式。这就像用更强大的新工具，去破解物理学里一个长久以来的超级难题。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
理论物理学家发布新研究，用AI算出了引力子的关键数据。

[翻成人话]
科学家们借助强大的人工智能程序，成功计算出了描述引力在最小尺度上如何运作的一些关键公式，这在以前非常困难。

[为什么重要]
因为引力如何与量子世界结合是物理学最大的未解之谜，这项研究提供了新的计算方法和结果，并且证明了AI能成为科学家探索前沿理论的得力助手。

[下一步确认点]
需要关注后续其他研究团队能否重复验证这一结果，以及该方法是否能推广到更复杂的计算中，从而真正推动量子引力理论的发展。

title: Understanding AI and learning outcomes
summary: OpenAI introduces the Learning Outcomes Measurement Suite to assess AI’s impact on student learning across diverse educational environments over time.
content: OpenAI introduces the Learning Outcomes Measurement Suite to assess AI’s impact on student learning across diverse educational environments over time
fullText: OpenAI introduces the Learning Outcomes Measurement Suite to assess AI’s impact on student learning across diverse educational environments over time
aiSummary: OpenAI推出学习成果测量套件，旨在评估AI对学生学习成果的长期影响。该工具将追踪AI在不同教育环境中的应用效果，帮助教育工作者量化技术干预的实际成效，为个性化教学提供数据支持。
aiInterpretation: OpenAI近日发布了名为“学习成果测量套件”的新工具，旨在系统性地评估人工智能在不同教育环境中对学生学习成果的长期影响。该套件将追踪AI技术应用与学生学业表现之间的关联，帮助教育者、研究者和政策制定者更准确地理解AI教育的实际效果。 为什么重要：随着AI在教育领域的应用日益广泛，从智能辅导系统到个性化学习平台，其实际教学效果却缺乏长期、系统的实证数据支持。该测量套件的推出，标志着行业开始从单纯的技术部署转向注重结果验证和效能评估，对于推动负责任、有证据支持的AI教育应用至关重要。 影响面： - **技术**：推动教育AI从功能开发转向效果验证，可能催生更注重学习成效的算法模型和评估标准。 - **商业**：为教育科技公司提供了评估产品有效性的新工具，可能影响其产品研发重点和市场竞争力。 - **产业链**：可能催生围绕AI教育效果评估的新服务生态，如第三方审计、数据分析咨询等。 - **用户/社会**：帮助学生、家长和教育工作者更清晰地了解AI工具的实际价值，促进教育资源的有效利用，并可能影响教育公平的讨论。 - **监管/安全**：为教育监管机构提供了评估和规范AI教育产品的潜在数据基础，有助于未来相关政策的制定。
aiExplanation: 简单说，OpenAI做了一个新工具，专门用来长期跟踪和测量AI辅助学习到底有没有用、有多大用，帮大家看清楚AI在教育里的真实效果。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
OpenAI发布了一个专门用来测量AI对学生学习效果影响的新工具包。

[翻成人话]
就是做个“尺子”，长期量一量用了AI辅导后，学生的学习成绩到底进步了多少。

[为什么重要]
因为现在学校里用AI越来越普遍，但到底管不管用、有多大用，一直缺乏可靠的长期数据。这个工具能帮大家弄明白AI教育的真实效果，避免盲目使用。

[下一步确认点]
需要关注这个工具具体如何测量、首批合作的教育机构有哪些，以及它得出的早期数据是否会公开分享。

title: How Axios uses AI to help deliver high-impact local journalism
summary: Axios COO Allison Murphy explains how the company uses AI to support local reporters, streamline newsroom workflows, and deliver high-impact local journalism at scale.
content: Axios COO Allison Murphy explains how the company uses AI to support local reporters, streamline newsroom workflows, and deliver high-impact local journalism at scale
fullText: Axios COO Allison Murphy explains how the company uses AI to support local reporters, streamline newsroom workflows, and deliver high-impact local journalism at scale
aiSummary: Axios首席运营官艾莉森·墨菲阐述了公司如何利用人工智能支持地方记者、简化新闻编辑室工作流程，并大规模提供有影响力的地方新闻报道。通过AI工具，记者能更高效地处理数据和信息，将精力集中于深度调查和社区故事，旨在解决地方新闻业资源紧张的普遍挑战。
aiInterpretation: 新闻媒体Axios的首席运营官艾莉森·墨菲详细介绍了公司如何将人工智能技术整合到地方新闻的生产流程中。其核心应用是使用AI工具辅助记者进行信息搜集、数据初步处理及内容分发，从而优化编辑部的工作流。这使记者能从繁琐的日常任务中解放出来，将更多时间投入到需要人类判断力的深度报道和社区故事挖掘上。 为什么重要：在传统地方新闻机构普遍面临预算削减和人员短缺的背景下，利用AI提升新闻生产的效率与规模至关重要。它为解决地方新闻“荒漠化”问题提供了一种技术驱动的可行路径，有助于维持对社区至关重要的监督性报道和信息服务。 影响面： - 技术：推动了自然语言处理、数据挖掘等技术在新闻生产场景中的具体应用与迭代。 - 商业：为新闻机构探索可持续商业模式提供了可能，通过降本增效来应对营收压力。 - 产业链：可能改变新闻内容的生产与分发环节，影响上下游如通讯社、内容平台的角色。 - 用户/社会：最终目标是让本地社区居民获得更及时、更相关的高质量新闻，强化社区信息纽带。 - 监管/安全：引发了关于新闻真实性、算法偏见以及AI生成内容伦理的新一轮行业讨论与潜在监管关注。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是一家新闻公司用电脑程序（AI）来帮记者干活，比如快速整理资料，这样记者就能专心写更重要的本地新闻，让社区消息更灵通。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
新闻媒体Axios正在用人工智能工具帮助它的地方记者更高效地工作。

[翻成人话]
他们用智能软件帮记者处理找资料、整理数据这些费时间的杂活，好让记者腾出手来，专心挖掘和撰写对本地社区真正重要的新闻故事。

[为什么重要]
因为很多地方报社缺钱缺人，快撑不下去了。用AI提高效率，是让这些关乎每个人日常生活的本地新闻能继续活下去、做下去的一个新办法。

[下一步确认点]
需要观察AI辅助写的新闻质量到底如何，会不会有错误或偏见，以及这种方式能不能真的帮地方新闻机构赚到钱，持续运营下去。
